items.name: "Eau D'Ogre" Perfume
items.note: Someone has managed to cram all the stench of an Ogre into one reeking bottle. Why? The world may never know. No human should ever willingly apply this to their body or attire. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Adamantine Dust
items.note: This is a small jar of Adamantine dust, most likely the end result of exposing a Drow item to the light of day. It seems the previous owner wasn't quite ready to let go of it. (Source: Baldur's Gate II)

items.name: Ancient Sword
items.note: A used and slightly rusted double-bladed sword. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Andar Gem
items.note: Andar is hard and durable, yielding translucent gems that flash green-red or brown-red when properly faceted. (Source: Baldur's Gate)

items.name: Banded Agate Gem
items.note: A round translucent white gemstone with a ring of striped brown through its center. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Barrel o' Eels
items.note: A large barrel of pickled eels. The barrel can be re-used, but it's unlikely the smell of pickled eel will ever truly be washed out. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Barrel o' Nails
items.note: A newly crafted barrel filled with iron nails. Might be difficult for the players to get this around if they don't have a cart. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Beaver Pelts
items.note: A string of eight beaver pelts. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Belt
items.note: An old leather belt. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Black Velvet Mask
items.note: An ornate black velvet mask sitched with silver thread. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Blank Scroll
items.note: A blank scroll. It could have any spell in the world on it, eventually. The possibilities are endless. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Blanket
items.note: A thick woolen blanket. Something like this is perfect for wrapping up during those long cold nights spent staring at a cave wall. (Source: PHB)

items.name: Blood Stained Dress
items.note: A blood-stained dress which, despite being found in ruins which have lain undisturbed for centuries, is still a vibrant, stunning blue. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Blood-Soaked Book
items.note: A blood-soaked book; a partially written history about a long-lost keep on the borderlands. The last written page is done in a different hand, and is scrawled with “The rules have changed!” in large, bold letters. Roughly a third of the book (of 100 pages) has been used. (Source: Unknown)

items.name: Blue Quartz Gem
items.note: A small quartz gemstone shaped in a bevelled square. It is transparent pale blue in color. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Blue Scarf
items.note: A finely woven blue wool scarf. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Boat Oar
items.note: Made for paddling boats, but it was made sturdy enough to fight strong currents. Maybe it's useful for self-defense in a pinch. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Bone Butter Churn
items.note: A butter churn made of bone slats, bound with hoops of silver. A scrimshaw scene depicting a crowd of human villagers beating a Halfling with shovels and hoes wraps around it. (Source: Unknown)

items.name: Bone Dice
items.note: A pair of engraved bone dice. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Bone Fragments
items.note: Bits of bone from an unknown creature. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Bone Needle
items.note: A long needle carved out of bone. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Broken Sword
items.note: This is simply a regular sword that has been partially broken, in combat or however. Most people would just get rid of it, but maybe this sword has a nice hilt or sentimental value. (Source: D&D Wiki)

items.name: Candle
items.note: Six red wax candles. (Source: PHB)

items.name: Carved Beads
items.note: A string of wooden carved beads, tied into a bracelet. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Chalk
items.note: A small piece of chalk used to draw on any hard surface. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Cloth Scrap
items.note: An old scrap of cloth from a shirt or sheet of some kind. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Cloth-of-gold Vestments
items.note: These holy garments are made of silk interwoven with gold threads. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Club
items.note: A crude club made to clobber animals and people alike, carved roughly from a thick cedar branch. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Comb
items.note: A small wooden comb. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Cooking Pot
items.note: A cast iron cooking pot. It smells like it was last used for soup. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Copper Acorns
items.note: Heavy, solid copper acorns with perfect natural detail. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Copper Chalice
items.note: A small copper chalice with a silver filigree. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Copper Ring
items.note: A simple piece of jewlery. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Crowbar
items.note: Using a crowbar grants advantage to Strength checks where the crowbar’s leverage can be applied. (Source: PHB)

items.name: Crude Box
items.note: A wooden box. Carved by someone who has had no formal training in woodworking, and it shows. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Crude Holy Symbol
items.note: A crudely carved wooden holy symbol. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Crude Pipe
items.note: A crudely carved wooden pipe. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Curious Powder Box
items.note: Metal box of unidentifiable powder. (Source: Unknown)

items.name: Dagger
items.note: A simple steel dagger with an oak handle and matching leather sheath. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Dart
items.note: A small, easy to conceal missile weapon. They might not do a lot of damage on their own but when your opponent has three of these embedded in their face before they can get within 10 feet of you they might reconsider the fight. (Source: PHB)

items.name: Dead Fey
items.note: A long-dead fey, dried and petrified as a trophy and hanging from ceiling. (Source: Unknown)

items.name: Dead Rat
items.note: An old dead rat. It has begun to decay, and eminates an unpleasant smell. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Deer Bust
items.note: The mounted head of a deer, it's impossibly massive horns have broken in places. (Source: Unknown)

items.name: Demon's Vale Wine
items.note: A one-gallon cask of Demon’s Vale Wine; a fiery red wine known to be made from hot peppers. (Source: Unknown)

items.name: Dice (6 Sided)
items.note: A six sided dice. Probably used in some silly tabletop game. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Dinosaur Bacon
items.note: 18 dried rashers of meat, possibly the largest rashers you have ever seen. They are wrapped in a sheep bladder cloth which has been labelled "THUNDERER MEAT" in large, rough handwriting. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Dire Bat Wings
items.note: Hanging from a drying rack are two large Dire Bat wings. It is unclear how long they have been seperated from the rest of the bat, but they are in good condition and could potentially be used as a crafting material. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Disk of Balance
items.note: A disk of finely polished stone. On one side, the Elvish rune for death is inscribed in blackest obsidian. Upon the other, the Dwarven rune for life is inscribed in the palest ivory. (Source: Unknown)

items.name: Dog Treats
items.note: These tender, juicy morsels contain rare seasonings with taste frequencies too high for human tongues. (Source: Fable II)

items.name: Dried Flower
items.note: A flower that has been dried out to preserve it. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Dust Ruby
items.note: A ruby the size of a man’s fist which sparkles and gleams with the promise of untold wealth in the faintest of lights, but which crumbles to dust upon the lightest touch. (Source: Unknown)

items.name: Electrum Bracer
items.note: An archer’s bracer of leather and electrum. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Empty Vial
items.note: An empty vial, just waiting to be filled with something more valuable than itself. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Eye Agate Gem
items.note: This simple agate gem has translucent circles of white dotted around its surface which bring to mind the image of an eye. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Farming Hoe
items.note: A farming tool primarily used for tilling fields. Its fine craftsmanship is sturdy enough to withstand backbreaking fieldwork, but its battle applications are untested. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Farming Pitchfork
items.note: A farming tool used to collect hay efficiently. It's light enough to be used by anyone. The four prongs are very sharp. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Fat Cat Statue
items.note: A life sized copper statue of a fat cat with amber eyes that purrs when stroked. Curiously, it does not detect as magic. (Source: Unknown)

items.name: Fencing Sword
items.note: A sword that has been constructed out of two planks from a wooden fence. (Source: OneManBland)

items.name: Fine Cheese
items.note: A 5 inch diameter wheel of fine, aged cheese. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Fishing Harpoon
items.note: A fisherman's tool that excels at catching large fish. Its particularly sharp spearhead makes it valuable as a weapon as well. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Fishing Tackle
items.note: Useful for catching fish. (Source: PHB)

items.name: Flesh Shield
items.note: A small wooden shield bound in human skin bearing a tattoo that appears to be some sort of map. The skin is held in place with a rim of beaten copper. (Source: Unknown)

items.name: Flint & Steel
items.note: A carved steel striker and a piece of shaped flint that allows the user to start a fire. (Source: PHB)

items.name: Fool's Gold
items.note: A handheld water pan with a number of chunks of pyrite in it, otherwise known as "Fool's Gold". You might be able to convince someone that these nuggets are real gold, but not for very long. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Fragrant Torches
items.note: A bag containing 1d8 torches infused with pleasant fragrances. This may have been owned by a tanner, to cover the overwhelming stench of tannin that is used in the leathermaking process. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Frolee's Guide to Follicle Perfection
items.note: A wizard called Frolee appears to have mass produced a five page guide to mustaches and beards. It has a unique, hairy cover made out of bearskin. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Frying Pan
items.note: A heavy cast iron frying pan. This can be used for frying, searing, browning and whacking people over the head! It has the remnants of this morning's breakfast smeared around it's rim. Damage is doubled when wielder has proficiency with cooking. (Source: D&D Wiki)

items.name: Garlic
items.note: A wreath of eight garlic buds. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Garotte Wire
items.note: The Garrote Wire can only be used on a medium or small creature while the user has advantage on the attack roll. On hit, the target takes 1d4 slashing damage and is grappled. Until the grapple ends, the target cannot breath and begins to choke, and the user has advantage on attack rolls against it. (Source: u/LeBlondes)

items.name: Gem
items.note: A teal gem. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Gem of Azurite
items.note: A small Azurite gem. It is opaque and a deep mottled blue in colour. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Glass of Magnification
items.note: A circle of warped glass attached to a small wooden handle. Strangely, when you look through the glass, everything appears to be bigger. Its not clear what kind of enchantment causes this effect. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Glass Sphere
items.note: A 14 inch diameter sealed glass sphere containing water and an ornamental fish. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Glittering Glass
items.note: A sparkling handful of raw gems. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Glitternight Dust
items.note: A tiny jar of glitternight dust. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Glow Stone
items.note: A small green stone that gives off a feint light that's practically imperceptable in anything other than pitch black darkness. It could be used as a marker or a light source in a pinch.

items.name: Goat Jerky
items.note: A pound of goat jerky wrapped in a hankerchief. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Gold Bracelet
items.note: A small gold bracelet. It's design is plain so as to be appropriate for anyone. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Gold Locket
items.note: A charming golden locket with a painted portrait inside. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Greatclub
items.note: A huge wooden greatclub, made from an old oak branch that has been carved and sanded into the shape of a large nail, with the narrower end wrapped in leather as a crude handle. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Hammer
items.note: A crosspeen hammer forged from steel. It has a gently crowned flat face that has been hardened and tempered for toughness. The handle is hand carved ash, flame treated and oiled. (Source: PHB)

items.name: Hand Mirror
items.note: A delicate oval of polished silver frame mirrored glass, its surface worked into flowing patterns that resemble vines caught mid‑bloom. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Handaxe
items.note: A straight wooden handle with a basic steel axe head on one end. It has been balanced well enough to use as a throwing axe. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Hematite
items.note: A rather dull gray-black hematite gem with the remains of a necklace fasten on its back. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Holy Bones
items.note: A reliquary containing the bones of an ancient holy man (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Holy Book
items.note: An old tome, written for the clergy of Pelor. It has a stiff leather dust jacket which has been worn down on the spine and a broken clasp once used to attach a chain (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Holy Symbol
items.note: A wooden holy symbol. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Honey
items.note: A quart of honey in a small glass bottle. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Honeysuckle Potion
items.note: A small, crystal vial of a clear, viscous liquid that smells strongly of honeysuckle. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Hope Chest
items.note: A delicately carved hope chest of polished cherry wood. The box contains nothing but a remarkably fine, gray sand drawn from some unknown locale. Beneath the new moon, this sand glows a faint blue. (Source: Unknown)

items.name: Hourglass
items.note: Source: PHB

items.name: Inhalation Herbs & Essences
items.note: Inhalation herbs & essences provide natural healing remedies for all manner of symptoms such as headaches, colds and flu.

items.name: Ink
items.note: A vial of black ink, for use with a quill. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Iron Buckle
items.note: An iron belt buckle that's beginning to rust. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Iron Earring
items.note: An iron earring. Missing it's twin. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Iron Nail
items.note: A large iron nail, useful for fixing things together. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Iron Spike
items.note: A large iron spike, useful for fixing heavy items together. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Iron Torc
items.note: An iron bracelet with six small silver figurines: A hare, a horse, a spear, a wolf’s head, a simple circle, and an oak tree. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Ivory Peg Leg
items.note: Carved from a single, sweeping piece of aged whale ivory, the prosthetic has a smooth, organic texture. The material’s natural grain is akin to soft waves of cream, honey, and pale grey. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Jar o' Eyeballs
items.note: A jar containing six eyeballs (2 human, 1 goblin, 1 ogre, 1 worg, and 1 hawk) in a clear liquid. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Javelin
items.note: A long thin shaft armed with a leaf-shaped head. It has a tassled based, to allow the javelineer to retrieve it after it has been thrown. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Jerky
items.note: A small satchel containing half a pound of jerked meat. It has a distinctly Goblin-like aroma that might raise suspicion to the picky eater. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Keg o' Nails
items.note: A 35 pound keg of iron nails. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Knee Breeches
items.note: A pair of bear fur knee breeches, slightly worn from having been used when horse riding. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Lantern
items.note: A slightly rusty old lantern with a glass casing. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Lapis Lazuli Gem
items.note: A gem of lapis lazuli, dark blue in colour with light yellow flecks. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Large Pile of Coal
items.note: A large pile of coal. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Lead Coins
items.note: A satchel of gold painted lead coins. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Leather Ball
items.note: A leather ball stuffed with chicken feathers. On a DC 15 peception check the player can find an electrum ring amongst the feathers. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Leather Quiver
items.note: A quiver shaped from thick, well‑oiled hide. Its cylindrical body is firm enough to protect arrows yet supple enough to flex slightly with movement. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Leather Satchel
items.note: A satchel crafted from thick, full‑grain hide. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Light Crossbow
items.note: An ash crossbow with a wooden shaft. This crossbow has been designed for smaller, foot long bolts, and is a comfortable ranged weapon for any sized creature. (Source: SRD)

items.name: Light Hammer
items.note: A small sledge hammer with a haft made of wood about one and a half feet long. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Little Riding Hood
items.note: A bright red hooded cape sized for a child, spattered with dried blood. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Lynx Eye Gem
items.note: Lynx eye is a special type of labradorite. It is pale to dark grey and has patches of coloured reflections. This "flash" is most commonly blue but can be of all shades. "Green Flash" labradorite is called Lynx Eye in the local regions. (Source: Baldur's Gate)

items.name: Lynx Eye Gem
items.note: Lynx eye is a specific type of labradorite (a feldspar gemstone). Labradorite as a class of stones is pale to dark gray and has patches of colored reflections. This "flash" is most commonly blue but can be of all shades. "Green Flash" labradorite is called lynx eye in the Realms. (Source: Baldur's Gate II)

items.name: Mace
items.note: This mace is essentially just a wooden shaft with a flanged iron head mounted on the end. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Magical Ring of Engagement
items.note: Someone used this to get married, and for them it really was a magical moment. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Malachite Gem
items.note: A large malachite gem. It is striated light and dark green across its polished surface. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Man Mask
items.note: A mask carved to look like the face of a man, but which seems to have been fitted to be worn by something with a sharp angled face. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Marbles
items.note: A handful of marbles in a small cloth bag. Equal to playing marbles is scattering them across the floor in an effort to trip your enemies. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Mechanical Mice
items.note: Three little mechanical mice found scurrying across the floor. They seem to be a benign presence – lacking even the slightest pestilent qualities of their flesh-and-blood counterparts. Who their creator was, how they are fueled and what purpose they serve (if any) are mysteries which may never be solved. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Milk Chocolate
items.note: Sometimes, nothing but chocolate will do, even if it's this inexpensive and slightly chalky variety. (Source: Fable II)

items.name: Mistletoe
items.note: A handful (D6) of mistletoe sprigs. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Moss Agate Gem
items.note: A beautiful moss agate gem. It is a translucent pink with a mossy gray texture. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Mudbrick Chocolate
items.note: Well, it's brown, but that doesn't necessarily make it chocolate. Still, at least its sweet. In an unusual and repugnant way. (Source: Fable II)

items.name: Fishing Net
items.note: An unused fishing net made out of spun mammoth hairs. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Obsidian Gem
items.note: A small opaque black gem. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Oil
items.note: A flask of oil suitable for use in a lantern. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Oily Rags
items.note: Three oily rags in an old wicker basket. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Old Hat
items.note: A ragged old hat with a moth eaten brim. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Old Pouch
items.note: An old pouch. Works as any pouch might, but it has a hole in it. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Old Round Shield
items.note: An old round shield. Beneath the boss is the dwarven script for "Valiant conduct by the bearer may draw the eye of the gods". (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Old Saddlebag
items.note: A rotten saddlebag with a few coins spilling out. The copper coins have some verdigris and the silver coins are badly tarnished. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Old Wheelbarrow
items.note: A rotting wheelbarrow holding a large number of burned out torches. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Parchment
items.note: A spare bit of parchment. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Peg Leg
items.note: You'll never know if the owner willingly parted with this prosthetic. You do know that it makes an effective cudgel. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Pick Axe
items.note: A straight wooden handle with a curved iron pick useful for breaking up rocky surfaces or hard earth. It would not make for an effecitve weapon, but without the head the handle would make for a rudimentary club. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Pickaxe
items.note: A pickaxe is the typical tool of a miner. Generally, they are used for excavating rocks and gemstones. However, in a pickle, they can be a very useful weapon. With two pointed edges, they are very good at ripping into enemies. (Source: D&D Wiki)

items.name: Pickle
items.note: A single pickle wrapped in an oily paper bag. It has a strong distinct smell, but otherwise seems like a normal pickle. (Source: Unknown)

items.name: Playing Cards
items.note: A classic 52 card set of playing cards. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Potpourri
items.note: A fragrant bowl of potpourri. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Prickaxe
items.note: A pickaxe has a curved iron head that is useful for breaking up rocky surfaces. A prickaxe has an uncomfortable knobbly handle and the axe head constantly falls off. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Primitive Hand Axe
items.note: A simple handaxe made from a smooth tree branch and a sharp piece of knapped flint. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Quarterstaff
items.note: A length of stoud wood ranging six to nine feet in length and shod with metal at both ends. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Raisins
items.note: Half a tin of raisins. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Rhodochrosite Gem
items.note: This rhodochrosite gem is a light pink colour and shaped to resemble a small heart. (Source: DMG)

items.name: River Stone
items.note: A stone that has been worn down to a smooth finish. Probably from a river bed. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Rope
items.note: 100 feet of rope, coiled neatly. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Rusty Horseshoe
items.note: An old rusty horseshoe, the metal may be worth something. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Sack of Flower Bulbs
items.note: A sack of prized flower bulbs

items.name: Sales Receipt
items.note: Receipt for delivery of slaves

items.name: Sausages
items.note: Package of d6 sausages

items.name: Saw
items.note: A simple saw blade forged from darkened iron that bears the marks of age: patches of oxidation, faint pitting, and the soft sheen that comes only from years of sharpening and use. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Scroll Case
items.note: A long, cylindrical container made from smooth wood, designed to shield delicate parchment from weather, wear, and wandering hands. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Service Record
items.note: A scroll detailing the bound service of two men-at-arms sworn to six months of service.

items.name: Shortbow
items.note: A small bow used by travelers and adventurers alike for protection. It doesn't do a lot of damage, but it can be used to attack foes from a distance. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Sickle
items.note: A farming tool that can be used as a weapon if needed. It has a long curved blade attached to a short wooden handle. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Silk Handkerchief
items.note: This silk handkerchief is embroidered with a pale pink pattern. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Silver Brooch
items.note: A silver brooch depicting a mage’s tower. (This could be a pass for magical wards at the mage’s tower, at the DM’s discretion.)

items.name: Silver Chalice
items.note: A finely made silver chalice etched in arcane symbols. An arcane check will find no trace of magic, however, and it may be that these are simply aesthetic. (Source: Unknown)

items.name: Silver Ewer
items.note: A large jug with a wide mouth coated in silver. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Silver Spoons
items.note: A set of 8 silver spoons.

items.name: Silver-tipped Quill
items.note: A silver-tipped quill with an elaborate white feather. (Source: Unknown)

items.name: Sling
items.note: A basic a leather strap with a pouch for holding the missile. The weapon is held by both ends of the strap and twirled around the wielder's head. Note: Always be careful not to knock yourself out. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Small Mirror
items.note: A slightly dirty mirror set in a painted wooden frame. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Snail Dye
items.note: A 1 quart jar of rare purple snail dye. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Soap
items.note: Some fresh, waxy soap. It smells faintly of lavender. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Soup Ladel
items.note: A kitchen implement often used for serving delicious soups. It was carved from the wood of a sturdy tree, so it actually packs quite the wallop. (Source: BOTW)

items.name: Spade
items.note: A spade is a simple tool designed for digging soil, the head can be made of any material that can hold its shape, but is most frequently made of metal to facilitate cutting into soil. (Source: D&D Wiki)

items.name: Spear
items.note: One of man's earliest weapons, dating back to the most primitive of times. A simple wooden pole with a steel spearhead on the end. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Spork of Positive Reinforcement
items.note: A spork carved from a single piece of maple hardwood. Part spoon, part fork, and entirely happy to be here. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Steel Chain
items.note: A steel chain measuring 1 foot long. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Steel Lockbox
items.note: A small metal chest with a lock, crafted from polished steel. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Straw Broom
items.note: A straw broom

items.name: Tactical Defense Stick
items.note: A sturdy wooden stick with two outcropping knobs that look like the crossguards of a sword. Cloth wrappings cover one end to form a more comfortable handle. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Threatening Probe
items.note: Has no function, but glows and vibrates in a way that suggests it could be used for distributing extreme pain.

items.name: Thunderstone Amulet
items.note: A small chain with flint stone in the shape of a triangle hanging from it. Popular tradition states that this is a "Thunderstone", a rock that has fallen from the sky during a storm. The fact that is resembles a primitive axe head, however, may fall upon deaf ears. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Tiger Eye Gem
items.note: Translucent brown with a bright golden center. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Tin Cup
items.note: A tin drinking cup. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Tinderbox
items.note: A full tinderbox. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Tobacco
items.note: A small amount of tobacco wrapped in a large soft leaf. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Torch
items.note: A short stick with oil-soaked cloth wrapped around one end so it can burn as a torch. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Turquoise Gem
items.note: Turquoise is an opaque aqua stone with darker mottling, and is found in the more arid reaches of the Realms. Horsemen will often place a sliver of this stone in a horse's harness as a sign of good luck. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Two-Sided Die
items.note: This perculiar dice is made out of copper and has two sides. Engraved on one side is the portrait of a long forgotten ruler, on the other is an ornate two headed chimera. Useful for making decisions. Doesn't roll well. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Unreadable Note
items.note: A paper note that's been left on a damp floor. It's completely illegible. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Used Whetstone
items.note: An old whetstone and a half-used vial of oil. The stone itself is ever-so-slightly worn into a curve from years of use, but it will be decades before it's wear renders it useless. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Vanishing Ring
items.note: A simple silver ring that, when looked at in bright light, doesn't seem to be entirely opaque. (Source: /u/SPYROHAWK)

items.name: Walking Stick
items.note: A wooden walking stick. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Wasplight Lantern
items.note: A dozen glow wasps in a round wire cage with handle. Equal to a torch light at night. Require food and water daily. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Waterskin
items.note: A waterskin made from a sheep's bladder. (Source: PHB)

items.name: Weight Training Equipment
items.note: The party finds a complete set of bronze and carved stone weight training equipment issued to the 4th army of some Ancient Empire. There is an inscription in one of the weights that reads "Dost thou even lift, brother?" (Source: Unknown)

items.name: Weighted Die (6 Sided)
items.note: A weighted dice. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Whistle
items.note: A small wooden whistle. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Wolf Pelt
items.note: A grey and white wolf pelt. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Wolfsbane
items.note: D8 springs of wolfsbane

items.name: Woodcutter's Axe
items.note: A woodcutter's tool of choice for felling trees. Its formidable weight and uneven balancing make it a slow, inefficient weapon. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Wooden Board
items.note: A weather worn wooden board, inscribed with patterns which make it clear that it was once used for a game whose rules have long since been lost to time.

items.name: Wooden Mop
items.note: Just a mop to the untrained eye, it excels at tidying up the place. But it owes its sturdy construction to a true craftsman, so it actually has some combat merit. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Wooden Plate
items.note: A carved wooden plate. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Wooden Spike
items.note: A long wooden spike, used to fix soft items together or kill vampires while they sleep. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Wormrose Incense
items.note: Source: Uberbeard

items.name: Wormscrape
items.note: A narcotic made from the petal of a wormrose flower. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: "The Art of Potionmaking" by Zygmunt Brigge
items.note: A large, leather-bound tome has a large illustrated potion vial stitched into the front cover. Above the illustration, written in Dwarven is the title; "The Art of Potionmaking". Further examination reveals this to be a comprehensive guide to potionmaking written by a Dwarf called Zygmunt Brigge. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: A Dungeon of One's Own
items.note: A dusty scroll which contains a political essay written by a witch called Virginia Woolfsblood. The essay delves into the systemic oppression of witches and advocates for the magical education and liberation of women in general. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Abacus
items.note: A basic wooden abacus, designed for merchants and treasurers. It comes with a simple latched case to protect the counting beads from damage. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Acid (Vial)
items.note: A round glass container filled with a clear liquid. It has a paper label indicating in common that it contains acid, and to warning not to accidentally break the glass. (Source: PHB)

items.name: Agni Mani Necklace
items.note: A necklace patterened with Agni Mani. These ornamental stones were usually found in desert regions. In the Realms, the name applies specifically only to black tektite material. (Source: Baldur's Gate)

items.name: Alchemist's Fire
items.note: Source: PHB

items.name: Amethyst
items.note: A common, bright purple gemstone. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Ammo Belt
items.note: This shoulder belt is ideally suited for holding sling bullets, darts, arrows or bolts. (Source: Baldur's Gate II)

items.name: Among the Kobolds
items.note: A detailed account of an adventurer who lived in an Kobold tribe for a number of years. You get a sense that there may have been some embellishment at some points, but it is otherwise an interesting examination of their general nature. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Ancient Tome
items.note: A tome whose pages have been yellowed with age and used to press metallic leaves of gold, silver, platinum, brass, copper, and bronze. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Aquamarine Necklace
items.note: A thick copper necklace set with a small aquamarine. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Bale of Furs
items.note: A large bale of rare furs. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Battleaxe
items.note: A smooth wooden shaft topped with a large, curved steel head. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Bedroll
items.note: A sheepskin bedroll. (Source: PHB)

items.name: Bloodstone
items.note: This dark red-brown heliotrope can sometimes be mistaken for a Stone of Invisibility but this one bears no overt magical powers. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Blowgun
items.note: Source: Uberbeard

items.name: Bone Darts
items.note: 1d20 normal bone darts. They resemble fletched knitting needles, about 1 foot in length. Not used often by themselves, but rather as ammunition in a blowgun. Can be dipped in poison and used as an effective delivery device. (Source: D&D Wiki)

items.name: Bonesaw
items.note: The bonesaw was used to amputate body parts after the skin had already been cut through with the amputation knife. (Source: D&D Wiki)

items.name: Bookstand
items.note: A 5 ft. tall book stand in maple-wood, the front of which is carved into the likeness of three intertwined foxes.

items.name: Boomerang
items.note: This throwing weapon was originally used by forest-dwelling Orcs to hunt game. Its unique shape allows it to return after being thrown. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Boomerang Sword
items.note: A curved sword favored by lizardmen. It can be used to attack directly or can be thrown like a boomerang. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Brandistock
items.note: This peculiar spear-like weapon is popular among travelers and law enforcement. The brandistock's three-pronged head is retractable, which renders it indistinguishable from a quarterstaff (unless closely inspected). Extending the blades is instantaneous, but pulling them back requires you to spend an action. (Source: D&D Wiki)

items.name: Brass Chamber Pot
items.note: A brass chamber pot in which has been hidden 3 pieces of rosy quartz.

items.name: Brass Knuckles
items.note: A set of brass knuckles. Used, but in good condition. (Source: D&D Wiki)

items.name: Breastplate Armor
items.note: This armor consists of a fitted metal chest piece worn with supple leather. Although it leaves the legs and arms relatively unprotected, this armor provides good protection for the wearer's vital organs while leaving the wearer relatively unencumbered. (Source: SRD)

items.name: Burning Oil
items.note: Source: Uberbeard

items.name: Carnelian Gemstone
items.note: This is an opaque orange gemstone set in a small brooch. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Carthus Tome of Pyromancy
items.note: An old pyromancy tome from Carthus containing pyromancies crafted for battle. It gives the impression it might have fallen through from a different plane of existence. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Carved Bone Statuette
items.note: A carved ox bone female figure statue on a wooden base. The statue is 16" in height. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Carved Wooden Mantle
items.note: A carved wooden mantle, meant to be mounted above a fireplace, crafted of ash-wood and stained a medium brown. The left side is carved in images of demons and flames, the right in images of angels and air. The top is carved into an idyllic forest theme and has a moon above the left corner and a sun above the right.

items.name: Cat Pendant
items.note: An electrum pendant shaped like a curled-up cat.

items.name: Cestus
items.note: A reinforced gauntlet which protects the knuckles and transforms mere punches into lethal blows. This weapon cannot be disarmed. It takes an action to don or doff a cestus. (Source: D&D Wiki)

items.name: Chain Mail Armor
items.note: Made of interlocking metal rings, chain mail includes a layer of quilted fabric worn underneath the mail to prevent chafing and to cushion the impact of blows. The suit includes gauntlets. (Source: SRD)

items.name: Chain Shirt Armor
items.note: Made of interlocking metal rings, a chain shirt is worn between layers of clothing or leather. This armor offers modest protection to the wearer's upper body and allows the sound of the rings rubbing against one another to be muffled by outer layers. (Source: SRD)

items.name: Chalcedony Gemstone
items.note: This pale white gemstone has a waxy luster and is slightly translucent. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Chicken Eggs
items.note: A dozen chicken eggs; hollowed out and filled with white flour.

items.name: Child's Mittens and Hat
items.note: Rabbit-fur mittens and hat sized for a child.

items.name: Chrysoprase Gemstone
items.note: An apple-green gemstone consisting of a variety of chalcedony that contains nickel. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Citrine Gemstone
items.note: This golden gem has been expertly worked into an oval but has long since fallen out of whatever ring or brooch it was carved for. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Classy Shirt
items.note: A leather bag containing an embroidered black silk shirt with eight silver buttons.

items.name: Climbing Harness
items.note: A leather vest with climbing gear: 12 iron pitons, a hammer, 50’ of silk rope, a grappling hook, a set of bronze crampons, thick leather gloves, and a leather harness seat..

items.name: Cloak of Griffon Feathers
items.note: A cloak of griffon feathers set on black velvet.

items.name: Cloth Doll
items.note: A child’s cloth doll with a small silver brooch pinned to it in the shape of a butterfly. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Clothier's Dye
items.note: A small cask of yellow clothiers’ dye. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Cockatrice Statue
items.note: A life-sized copper and silver statuette of a cockatrice. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Colloquial Black Speech for Orcs, Trolls and Men
items.note: This book appears to be a phrasebook for those looking to learn the basics of the Orcish language. It has an entire section of swear words, which is most amusing. The book seems to have an effect on anyone using the phrase book to speak in Black Speech. They become prone to shouting louder and louder the less they are understood. Nobody knows why this is; the book is not magical in the slightest. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Combat Boomerang
items.note: This combat boomering does not resembe the conventional "V" shape of a normal boomerang and instead has more of a "tick mark" shape. One end has a smaller wing which is both thicker and carefully reinforced with metal studs. It can be used as both a ranged weapon as expected, but can also be used in close quarters as a club. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Copper Bars
items.note: Four bars of copper.

items.name: Copper Necklace
items.note: A copper necklace set with a small amulet shaped into a symbol representing the sun.

items.name: Copper Tongue Scraper
items.note: A copper tongue scraper

items.name: Cow
items.note: A cow. It's a real hoofer.

items.name: Crystal Vase
items.note: A large crystal vase, silver trimmed, with a dozen dead roses and some stagnant water within.

items.name: Dead Man's Plate
items.note: A suit of plate armor, worn by the skeletal remains of a long-dead warrior. The metal still glistens as if it had been polished yesterday. On inspection, the name of seemingly every warrior to ever don the armor has been inscribed upon the front and back of the chest plate, covering nearly every inch of its surface with precisely written, black runes. There remains room for one last name. (Source: Unknown)

items.name: Dehydrated Homunculi
items.note: A dried out and preserved homunculus. This one is a winged humanoid creature, similar to a small gargoyles or tiny demon. It's eye have been replaced with beads and it is stiff and unmoving.

items.name: Dire Barnacle
items.note: Three vicious little mollusks (1hp, Armour 1) that explode in a 2m blast (d8) when killed.

items.name: Door Rubble
items.note: The remnants of a massive door of stone which has been smashed into rubble. A magic mouth which had once adorned the door remains intact. As the PCs draw near it will issue its warning: “Disturb not this chamber, lest the world suffer.” Whatever the mouth was set to guard is gone – the chamber beyond the broken door is empty.

items.name: Double Axe
items.note: A normal hand axe, only there's two of them. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Double Spear
items.note: The double spear is a polearm comprising of a wooden haft with a short thrusting blade at either end. It can be used in one or two hands like a spear, but in the hands of a master both heads can be used effectively. (Source: D&D Wiki)

items.name: Dragon Banner
items.note: A battle pennant, triangular in shape, of a black dragon on red silk. The staff is a silver-tipped lance. (Pennant: 30gp, Lance: 25gp)

items.name: Dragon Head Shield
items.note: A large copper shield, embossed with a copper dragon’s head. “Elvalynte” is inscribed on the interior in a flowing script. (34gp)

items.name: Draining Dagger
items.note: A simple silver dagger with a blood line that runs down the center of the blade to drain its victim’s blood. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Drow Medallion
items.note: An electrum drow house-medallion bearing the image of a crimson scorpion with the house name inscribed on the back in the drow tongue (House Ulrather).

items.name: Drunkard's Hat
items.note: A simple straw hat. (Source: u/DngnMaster)

items.name: Dwarven Compass
items.note: A finely crafted compass of gold and silver, decorated and labelled with ornate, dwarven and draconic runes. Once every 1d4 hours it randomly changes the cardinal direction to which its arrow points.

items.name: Dwarven Hand Axe
items.note: A simple hand axe and belt sheath. The axehead is inlaid with dwarvish runes which reference the high quality of items made by Tomu Aethmalk, the blacksmith who crafted the axe. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Dwarven Holy Symbol
items.note: A dwarven holy symbol in silver; shaped into a flaming war-hammer set against an anvil-shaped backdrop.

items.name: Echo Crystal
items.note: A prismatic crystal which echoes back – in a deep, melodious voice – every word which is said around it two seconds after it has been said.

items.name: Electrum Rose
items.note: A detailed rose, with petals and thorns, constructed of silver and electrum.

items.name: Electrum Sheet
items.note: A large (18”x28”), rectangular electrum sheet with ancient writing on it. The writing, if deciphered, is a brief history of a powerful magical item known as the House of Winds, with several clues as to where the item may have been lost fourteen hundred years ago. The House of Winds is described as an ornate mithril helm of legendary power.

items.name: Elephant Tusks
items.note: A collection of six elephant tusks.

items.name: Elven Sword
items.note: The ornamentation that adorns this blade is a traditional elven design. It's forged from a very durable and rust-proof metal. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Fairy Wing Cage
items.note: A small wooden case holding four pairs of fairy wings.

items.name: Field Tent
items.note: A large, ornate field tent such as a lord would have, room for 12 persons

items.name: Fire Agate Gem
items.note: Fire Agate is the name given to chalcedony which contains thin lines of iridescent goethite. When properly cut, the iridescence displays red, brown, gold, and green hues, and the finest specimens are partly translucent. (Source: BGII)

items.name: Firestone Gem
items.note: This gem normally looks like quartz, but if allowed to absorb heat will change in color to resemble a bright orange garnet. When smashed against something, it is drained of color and will light that thing on fire

items.name: Flail
items.note: A specialized bludgeoning weapon consisting of a weighted, spiked, metal head attached to a handle by a flexible chain. This unique construction allows the wielder to generate immense kinetic energy through wide swinging arcs, making it exceptionally effective at bypassing shields and crushing armored foes. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Flask Bandolier
items.note: A bandolier holding 8 silver flasks (empty (x3), brandy (x2), acid (x1), ogre blood (x1), gold dust (x1, value of gold: 12gp). (Flasks: 3gp each, bandolier: 5gp, ogre blood (to alchemist): 12gp, acid: 10gp, brandy:

items.name: Flask Belt
items.note: Utility belt of flasks

items.name: Fluted Short Spear
items.note: A ceremonial short spear; ash wood with an iron point. Seven red and three blue dyed vulture feathers are attached to the head of the spear. The iron is fluted to whistle through the air, if thrown. (Source: Unknown)

items.name: Foul Ivory Statue
items.note: A life sized ivory carving of a chicken.

items.name: Frost Giant's Necklace
items.note: A frost giant necklace of silver upon which are pierced 17 human-sized hands. All of them appear to be the right hand of their former owner.

items.name: Frosted Dagger
items.note: A simple dagger with a blue hilt. The blade is almost always covered in frost. (Source: Arctiquaza)

items.name: Fungus Spores
items.note: Source: Uberbeard

items.name: Fur Basket
items.note: A wicker basket with a pair of mink furs within.

items.name: Glass Window
items.note: A small colored-glass window pane depicting the crowning of the first king.

items.name: Glowing Crystal
items.note: A miniature crystal – small enough to lie upon the outstretched finger of a halfling child – glows softly, and pulses whenever an object colored red is brought near.

items.name: Golden Horn
items.note: An eight inch golden horn, likely more of a ceremonial object than of any real value as an instrument.

items.name: Golden Orb
items.note: A roughly beaten golden orb, roughly 10 inches in diameter. The orb is etched with the likeness of an orcish skull with an axe embedded in it. This was used by an orcish chieftain as his “royal orb”.

items.name: Golden Signet Ring
items.note: A golden signet ring, origin unknown.

items.name: Grandfather's Broadsword
items.note: A grandfather’s broadsword, plain but quality. An unknown script runs along the inner side of the scabbard. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Great Bow
items.note: This 6-foot tall bow is made of elm rather than yew or ash, making it astonishingly stiff, large and strong, and equally capable of use for long and short shooting. (Source: PHB)

items.name: Great Spear
items.note: This mighty spear is favoured by centaurs. (Source: D&D Wiki)

items.name: Greataxe
items.note: A large curved handle is topped with a broad, thin blade designed to be effective against calvalry and shields. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Half Plate Armor
items.note: Half plate consists of shaped metal plates that cover most of the wearer's body. It does not include leg protection beyond simple greaves that are attached with leather straps. (Source: SRD)

items.name: Hand Crossbow
items.note: Source: Uberbeard

items.name: Hand Harp
items.note: Silver hand sized harp

items.name: Harpy Feather Fan
items.note: A hand-fan of clipped, black-dyed harpy feathers bound with copper wiring.

items.name: Heavy Crossbow
items.note: Source: Uberbeard

items.name: Hell Scented Potpourri
items.note: Potpourri with demon blood – invigorating & entrancing

items.name: Hemp Rope
items.note: Seven lengths of extremely thick, 30’ long, hemp rope, such as that used for catapults.

items.name: Hidden Daggers
items.note: A dozen quality daggers in a rolled up rug. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Hide Armor
items.note: This crude armor consists of thick furs and pelts. It is commonly worn by barbarian tribes, evil humanoids, and other folk who lack access to the tools and materials needed to create better armor. (Source: SRD)

items.name: Holy Sword of Pentorus
items.note: A broken holy sword, inscribed with the name of a legendary Paladin. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Holy Symbol
items.note: The holy symbol of a god long thought dead and gone.

items.name: Holy Water
items.note: Three vials of holy water.

items.name: How to Win Friends and Influence People
items.note: This book peddles a philosophy of personal ascendancy and manipulation by charisma alone. The authors and advocates of such tomes claim that, by drawing on the most recent theories in sociology and interpersonal psychology, it is possible to raise an individual's charisma by a single point. They're wrong, that can only be done by magic, but what you read is your own. (Source: BGII)

items.name: Iol Gem
items.note: Iol is actually short for Iolite (cordierite), although a common nickname is "violet stone" even though its overall hue is usually blue. Iols are usually cut into faceted gems to best display the stone's color change as it is viewed from different directions. Small, cut iols can be clear, but larger specimens usually contain silky inclusions of another substance such as hemitite crystals, which give the same rich golden flash of color as in sunstones. (Source: BGII)

items.name: Iron Flask
items.note: An iron flask holding a viscous liquid of intense, emerald-green that smells faintly of citrus fruit. The liquid is a concentrated, magical dye for clothing and the like, equal to four full barrels of normal dye. If consumed, it turns the drinker green for a full month.

items.name: Ivory Dress
items.note: A lovely ivory-colored dress of silk with flowing sleeves. The dress has tiny pearls sewn at the neckline.

items.name: Ivory Mice
items.note: A small sack containing 32 carved ivory mice

items.name: Jade Fountain
items.note: A decorative fountain, crafted elegantly from a single block of jade and decorated with gold leaf, in which the water flows in the wrong direction.

items.name: Jade Pendant
items.note: A small, white jade pendant without necklace. The pendant depicts a unicorn rampant with a cracked back leg.

items.name: Jallak's Shield
items.note: A small steel shield with a triangular shape. It is emblazoned with a griffon device in red on a field of gray. This battered shield is inscribed on the interior with the words: “May this shield be your protection in battle, Jallak. Honor the Galbrand name.” (Source: Unknown)

items.name: Jasper Gem
items.note: Jasper is an opaque quartz found in reds, browns, and blacks. Very rare specimens are blue or have bands of blue against the other colours. Jasper can be crushed and used in making potions and magical devices which protect against poison. (Source: Baldur's Gate)

items.name: Java Potion
items.note: Made from beans that were crushed to a fine powder and robbed of all but a trace of their natural essence, which would be criminal had the beans not been canephora rubbish to start with. (Source: Fable II)

items.name: Jewel Wood
items.note: A 6’ stack of 1” x 8” x 12’ planks of rare, scented jewel wood

items.name: Kilan Wester's Royal Recipes
items.note: A large tome filled with over one hundred recipes for wild boar, goose, and venison; written by Kilan Wester, a former royal chef (retired). (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Knife Vest
items.note: A padded vest of hardened leather, the breastplate of which has been fitted with 12 small sheaths designed for throwing knives. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Large Bronze Bell
items.note: A large (36” diameter) bronze bell.

items.name: Leather Armor
items.note: The breastplate and shoulder protectors of this armor are made of leather that has been stiffened by being boiled in oil. The rest of the armor is made of softer and more flexible materials. (Source: SRD)

items.name: Leather Bag of Dragon Scales
items.note: A leather bag containing 6 green dragon scales (young adult).

items.name: Leviathan Organ
items.note: Pulsating organ of leviathan (is sack/cloth)

items.name: Libatius Borage's Advanced Potion Making
items.note: A large tome which details advanced techniques in the field of potion making. It's dust cover is a little tatty but the pages inside have kept well and it still has plenty of life left in it. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Lich Skull
items.note: Fossilized skull of lich-arcane power source

items.name: Longbow
items.note: Source: Uberbeard

items.name: Longsword
items.note: A basic longsword made of well forged iron. It has a plain crossguard, leather wrapped handle and a rounded metal pommel with a blacksmith's mark pressed into it. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Lucky Coin
items.note: A simple golden coin. It is two sided and compared to most, has exceptional printing definition. On the obverse is depicted an unknown dragonborn ruler, and the reverse is decorated with a plume of songweed flowers. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Malachite Bracelet
items.note: A thin malachite bracelet with a silver clasp.

items.name: Malivar's Wardrobe
items.note: A large bronze wardrobe, banded in steel, which contains Violet Fungi (hp 15) and a human body with rotting noble’s clothing, a rusty dagger, and a thin platinum bracelet set with a moonstone.

items.name: Mallius Metallius, A Layman's Guide to Metallurgy
items.note: This tome has been bound in iron plates, giving the sense that it could take a real beating if needed. It's been well kept, however, and inscribed in the front is the title, "Mallius Metallius" with the subtitle, "A Layman's Guide to Metallurgy". (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Mammoth Sword
items.note: A sword of truly mammoth proportions. Resting within a chamber more than thirty meters long, the sword stretches from one end to the other. Whatever creature was meant to wield this mighty weapon would truly stagger the imagination of a dragon. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Manticora Branding Iron
items.note: A mithril-headed branding-iron bearing the sigil of the Manticora Trading House.

items.name: Manual of Bodily Health
items.note: A typical manual of bodily health provides the reader with valuable information regarding preventative medicine, basic first aid, and proper muscle toning. (Source: BGII)

items.name: Manual of Gainful Exercise
items.note: A typical Manual of Gainful Exercise provides the reader with a strictly regimented routine of daily stretches and tests designed to increase muscle bulk. (Source: BGII)

items.name: Manual of Quickness of Action
items.note: A typical manual of quickness of action provides the reader with a wide variety of tips and pointers regarding the motion and coordination of one's hands and feet. This small tome is much in demand by people from many walks of life, including rogues wishing to improve their slight-of-hand and paladins wanting to perfect their thrust and parry. (Source: BGII)

items.name: Masquerade Mask
items.note: A masquerade mask crafted of leather and peacock feathers.

items.name: Masterwork Gaming Darts
items.note: With an elegant hinged case of black onyx, the characters find a set of masterwork gaming darts. Although useless for combat, the darts will give a +2 skill check bonus to anyone using them to play a game of darts (due to their superb balance and construction).

items.name: Matroshka Seeds
items.note: Within a wooden box carved with pastoral scenes lies a leather purse, and within the purse are a handful of seeds. If these seeds are planted, they will take root and grow into plants of unnatural shape, hue, and life unlike anything seen upon this world, and operating by utterly alien principles.

items.name: Maul
items.note: Source: Uberbeard

items.name: Medicinal Herbs
items.note: A cabinet containing 24 glass jars of various medicinal herbs

items.name: Mineral Oil
items.note: 2 oz bottle of mineral oil

items.name: Moonstone
items.note: Moonstone is an opaque, white feldspar gem polished to a bluish sheen. Old legends say that the sight of a moonstone would cause a lycanthrope to revert to his animal form. Whether or not this is true is unknown as anyone close enough to find out apparently hasn't lived to tell the tale. However moonstones have been used in spells that effect this particular curse. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Moonstone Sheath
items.note: A dagger sheath of bronze, set with a small moonstone. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Moradin's Magical Bestiary
items.note: A bestiary!

items.name: Morningstar
items.note: Source: Uberbeard

items.name: Mysterious Ivory Figurine
items.note: An age-worn ivory figurine, which, nonetheless, bears an uncanny resemblance to a young woman the characters have just met. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Net
items.note: Source: Uberbeard

items.name: Nymph Statuette
items.note: A foot-high bronze statuette of a pair of entwined nymphs, dancing.

items.name: Old Boot
items.note: An old boot filled with chunks/flakes of silver-bearing quartz ore.

items.name: Onyx Gemstone
items.note: This gemstone has opaque bands of black and white across it's surface. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Orb Of Venom
items.note: Orb of venom-Applies to all of user attacks. Target takes an additional 1d4 poison damage on their next turn.

items.name: Orcish Cookbook
items.note: This appears to be a cookbook written by an unnamed Orc. It has some insightful notes on how best to cook Owlbear and a rather unpleasant section on what spices go well with human flesh. (Source: BGII)

items.name: Ornate Axe Head
items.note: An unhafted axe head of exceptional craftsmanship. It’s faces are inlaid with a faience scene showing a crowd of human villagers throwing a Halfling down a well.

items.name: Ornate Smoking Pipe
items.note: An ivory and copper smoking pipe with stale tobacco inside.

items.name: Ornate Wooden Leg
items.note: A wooden leg, carved to appear as a standing leopard with green agate eyes. (Agates: 4gp each; the whole leg, with eyes: 10gp)

items.name: Padded Armor
items.note: Padded armor consists of quilted layers of cloth and batting. (Source: SRD)

items.name: Pendulum
items.note: Source: Uberbeard

items.name: Phoenix Egg
items.note: "Phoenix Egg" from a peddler in the City of Brass. After consulting with a few scholars, he later found that (at least in my world lore) Phoenixes are born of primal energy and not hatched, and that he had in fact purchased a very large jar of Alchemist's Fire.

items.name: Pie Bombs
items.note: Three jet black spheres, about the size of an apple, that smell distinctly of cooked pastry. (Source: Unknown)

items.name: Piglet
items.note: A piglet.

items.name: Pike
items.note: Source: Uberbeard

items.name: Plate Armor
items.note: Plate consists of shaped, interlocking metal plates to cover the entire body. A suit of plate includes gauntlets, heavy leather boots, a visored helmet, and thick layers of padding underneath the armor. Buckles and straps distribute the weight over the body. (Source: SRD)

items.name: Poison Dagger
items.note: Refillable curved poison dagger (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Poisoned Throwing Needles
items.note: Pouch of 6 poisoned throwing needles. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Potion of Healing
items.note: A character who drinks the magical red fluid in this vial regains 2d4 + 2 hit points. Drinking or administering a potion takes an action. (Source: PHB)

items.name: Pouch
items.note: A cloth or leather pouch that can hold up to 20 sling bullets or 50 blowgun needles, among other things. (Source: PHB)

items.name: Pouch of Healing Potions
items.note: A large carry pouch made from the skin of a monkey. Inside are 1d6 Potions of Healing. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Priest Skeleton
items.note: A human skeleton in rotted priest’s robes wearing a ring and necklace. (Electrum ring: 4gp Silver necklace: 3gp)

items.name: Prism's Copper Goblin
items.note: A goblin skull that has been dipped in molten copper. (1gp)

items.name: Ram's Head Mace
items.note: A silver-headed heavy mace, the head of which is shaped into the likeness of a ram’s head. (Source: Unknown)

items.name: Random Monster Taxidermy
items.note: Random monster taxidermy

items.name: Rapier
items.note: Source: Uberbeard

items.name: Raven Statue
items.note: A life-size basalt statue of a raven.

items.name: Replica Castle
items.note: A 3’ square replica of a castle, in wood, complete with ramparts and miniature ballistae and toy soldiers. Scribbled in some spots are notes written in draconic that suggest a plan to assault the castle (“Kill guard here first”, “Murder holes overhead”, Fireball this area”, etc…)

items.name: Reversible Robes
items.note: Reversible, finely crafted robes

items.name: Ring Mail Armor
items.note: This armor is leather armor with heavy rings sewn into it. The rings help reinforce the armor against blows from swords and axes. Ring mail is inferior to chain mail, and it's usually worn only by those who can't afford better armor. (Source: SRD)

items.name: Ring of Fire Detection
items.note: Source: Gooch_Ticklr

items.name: Ring of the Adventurous Limb
items.note: If worn on a hand, the entire arm will vanish.  It will return after 24 hours have elapsed.  Roll a d10: 1 - tattoo on arm, 2 - arm injured, 3 - gained a ring, probably a wedding ring, 4 - ring missing, 5-10 - nothing special.  It will have a similar effect on legs.

items.name: Royal Documents
items.note: Collection of documents with traces of royal bloodlines

items.name: Sand Crown Crown
items.note: When used (worn), this seemingly golden crown turns to sand and falls to the ground where it reforms into a crown. Any gold, silver, or copper item it is placed near will also gain this property. A coin would only turn to sand when it was being used (spent), etc

items.name: Sardonyx Gemstone
items.note: Bands of white-yellow sard cross the surface of this dark onyx gemstone. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Satchel
items.note: A thick leather satchel containing an adventurer's’ kit: 6 wax candles, two pieces of white chalk, two pieces of red chalk, a small bag of (100) clay marbles, a whistle, four pieces of charcoal, three large sacks, six iron pitons, a hammer, a tinderbox with flint and steel, 2 empty copper vials, a chunk of coal, and three large sheets of paper.

items.name: Satyr Pipes
items.note: Silver pan/satyr pipes/flute

items.name: Scale Mail Armor
items.note: This armor consists of a coat and leggings (and perhaps a separate skirt) of leather covered with overlapping pieces of metal, much like the scales of a fish. The suit includes gauntlets. (Source: SRD)

items.name: Scimitar
items.note: Source: Uberbeard

items.name: Scroll Case
items.note: Nigh-indestructible scroll case – leviathan ivory, jewel encrusted, demon paintings on side – scraps of scroll inside

items.name: Scythe
items.note: This weapon is inspired by the common farming implement used to cut swaths of grown hay, but it has a straight handle and a heavier blade. Despite its humble origins, the scythe is recognized as a symbol of death in many cultures because of its use in reaping. (Source: D&D Wiki)

items.name: Sea Shell Coil
items.note: A 40’ coil of rare sea shells strung on a silken cord

items.name: Seimai's Guide to Healing Tonics
items.note: A relatively small compendium of information about healing tonics. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Shandon Gem
items.note: Shandon is another name for natrolite; its slender, colorless crystals yield tiny faceted gems used often in veils and robes in order to capture the beading effect of water glistening upon the material. (Source: BGII)

items.name: Sharkskin Armour
items.note: Studded leather armor made from sharkskin set with iron disks.

items.name: Sharpening Sheath
items.note: A dagger sheath of hardened leather, set with a number of cheap decorative stones. Any dagger kept within this sheath is automatically sharpened as if it were a fresh blade. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Sharpening Stone
items.note: A high quality sharpening stone

items.name: Shield
items.note: A shield is made from wood or metal and is carried in one hand. (Source: SRD)

items.name: Shortsword
items.note: Source: Uberbeard

items.name: Shurikens
items.note: A leather string holding 1d12 silver shurikens.

items.name: Silver Bullets
items.note: A pouch filled with 13 silver sling bullets. Each bullet is engraved with the holy symbol of Pelor. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Silver Hand Mirror
items.note: A silver hand mirror with mother-of-pearl backing.

items.name: Silver Ore
items.note: A large chunk of raw silver ore.

items.name: Silver Ring
items.note: A simple silver ring that bears no inscription or identifying marks on it's surface. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Silver Statuettes
items.note: A set of five small silver statuettes (each about 2” tall) consisting of a cat, an owl, a falcon, a horse and a griffon.

items.name: Six Fingered Gloves
items.note: Two six-fingered gloves, along with a matching seven-fingered glove. The set is made of supple black leather, and stitched with fine, silver thread.

items.name: Skeleton Key
items.note: A single, platinum skeleton-key on a platinum ring. The key is finely crafted, with a delicate design etched in elvish characters along the length and across the bow.

items.name: Skeleton Key Chain
items.note: Key chain with multiple types of ‘skeleton keys’

items.name: Skydrop Gem
items.note: Skydrop is the common name given to clear or lightly colored tektite material; fragments of glass of celestial (meteoric) origin, found in the vast shifting sands of Anauroch and other deserts. (Source: BGII)

items.name: Slave Order
items.note: A bearer-order allowing the possessor to take ownership of a dozen slaves from Zoealage the flesh-monger (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Sledge Hammer
items.note: This large sledge hammer has been cast entirely out of steel. There is a small symbol of a rat moulded into the hammer head, above Dwarvish lettering that indicates the weight. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Smoking Box
items.note: A long, narrow wooden box containing forty, ½ lb rolls of quality smoking leaf.

items.name: Smoking Pipe
items.note: A six foot long smoking pipe made of ivory, possibly a narwhal horn, carved to look like a twisting serpent.

items.name: Smuggler's Boots
items.note: Smugglers boots – great for hiding a concealed backup dagger or wand

items.name: Smuggler's Coat
items.note: A hooded cloak with a dozen inside pockets.

items.name: Smuggler's Dagger
items.note: A broad dagger with a hilt that can be unscrewed. The space revealed is sufficient to hold up to 25 coins, and currently holds 15 silver coins. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Snow Serpent Stockings
items.note: Four pairs of snow serpent fur felt stockings

items.name: Special Syrup
items.note: A two gallon stone ware jug containing hallucinogenic tree sap syrup

items.name: Spiked Chain
items.note: A length of spiked chain between 6 and 8-feet long with wicked barbs welded onto one end. (Source: PHB)

items.name: Splint Armor
items.note: This armor is made of narrow vertical strips of metal riveted to a backing of leather that is worn over cloth padding. Flexible chain mail protects the joints. (Source: SRD)

items.name: Stabby String
items.note: A popular Goblin trap; a 50 ft length of neatly coiled wire that has nails welded onto it at regular intervals. The resulting product can be used either by running it across the floor or by intertwining it along a fence or wall. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Star Diopside Gem
items.note: Star Diopside is the most prized form of a hard, durable gemstone that is rarely found in attractive colours. It is usually too dark for beauty, however mountain and streambed-pebble crystals of a pale green hue make attractive stones such as diopside. When a gem is found that is darker green it can be cut in such a fashion so to produce four- or six-rayed stars. (Source: BGII)

items.name: Star Rose Quartz
items.note: This gemstone is a polished pink sphere. When viewed at a specific angle with a source of light an asterism (star-effect) is visible across it's surface. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Steel Spiked Gauntlets
items.note: A pair of blue enameled steel spiked gauntlets. The palms of both gauntlets are designed to display the Cambersome family crest, a stylized ‘C’ shaped into the likeness of a coiled serpent.

items.name: Studded Leather Armor
items.note: Made from tough but flexible leather, studded leather is reinforced with close-set rivets or spikes. (Source: SRD)

items.name: Stuffed Falcon
items.note: A stuffed falcon, tattered with age, with black onyx eyes. (Onyx value: 5gp each)

items.name: Sunrod Quiver
items.note: A short quiver holding 3 sunrods.

items.name: Sunstone Gem
items.note: Sunstone is a feldspar jem, closely related to moonstone, and more properly known as oligoclase. Sunstone can be colorless or faintly greenish and of facet grade, but most common by far is its softer (cabochon) variety. This yields gemstones that have bright red or orange spangles (minute crystals) suspended in a nearly colorless background in a parallel fashion, giving the whole a rich golden or redish brown color. (Source: BGII)

items.name: Tasty Cake
items.note: A well-sealed cake box with intact cake

items.name: Tattoo Kit
items.note: Ritualistic tattooing kit

items.name: Tchazar Gem
items.note: Tchazar is the common name given to aragonite, a straw-yellow gemstone found in elongated, prism-shaped crystal form. It is soft and fragile, and requires skilled cutting to yield faceted gems.. (Source: BGII)

items.name: Teak Mask
items.note: A cracked mask of teak wood, colored in (chipped) blue and red paint depicting the face of a fairy (4sp)

items.name: Teakwood Quarterstaff
items.note: An ornately carved quarterstaff of teakwood. The end is carved into the likeness of a dragon’s head, with (painted red) flame issuing from its mouth and the butt end carved into an entwining serpent-like tail. (5gp) (Source: Unknown)

items.name: Terrible Thermometer
items.note: This thermometer has a long, silver blade and a pommeled wooden handle. "Come here, monster! I just want to take your temperature!" (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: The Book of Monster Recipes
items.note: This leather-bound recipe book appears to be aimed at adventurers who aren't satified with merely fighting monsters and taking their treasure. It features a mixed selection of recipies, with side-notes on how best to prepare the remains of each creature included. "Braized Dragon Liver and Onions", "Eye of the Beholder Soup", "Rust Monster Jerky" and "Gelatinous Cube Jelly" are particular standouts. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: The Headbanger
items.note: This impromptu club was once a lute, but has since been smashed and clumsily repaired many times. Perhaps the bard who originally owned this played a few too many of the wrong note. (Source: Fable)

items.name: The Neverburning Torch
items.note: The neverburning torch. A jet black torch, with an inscription in gold upon its side: “Only in your hour of darkest need will I light.” All attempts – magical or otherwise – to light the torch will fail, but if the character carrying the torch ever finds himself upon the brink of death, the torch will flare to life.

items.name: The Recipes and Ruminations of One Dradeel of Tethir
items.note: The Recipes and Ruminations of One Dradeel of Tethir is a book with food recipes from Dradeel. Even a casual look will inform the reader that he was a wizard with an addled mind. (Source: BGII)

items.name: The Three Rules to Poisoncraft
items.note: This tatty old tome is missing a number of pages, leaving whatever the third rule of Poisoncraft completely unknown. Hopefully you'll manage with the first two, unlike whomever owned this book before you. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Tigerseye Ring
items.note: You find a barrel filled with broken crockery. Near the bottom is a mouse nest in which, amidst the tangled mouse fur, lays a silver ring set with a polished tigerseye.

items.name: Tobacco Cask
items.note: A small cedar cask of dwarven pipe tobacco. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Tome of Clear Thought
items.note: A typical Tome of Clear Thought contains a collection of esoteric and scholarly literature, often involving obscure mathematical or algebraic formulae or the occasional reference to inter-planar biology.. (Source: BGII)

items.name: Tome of Understanding
items.note: A typical Tome of Understanding contains a sober, if dry analysis of legal battles, historic events, government policies, philosophical treatises and theories of magic and metaphysics. (Source: BGII)

items.name: Top Secret Battle Monkeys
items.note: Box of ‘Top Secret Battle Monkeys’

items.name: Tourmaline Chain
items.note: A delicate silver chain set with a tourmaline.

items.name: Treasure Map I
items.note: Piece of Legendary weapon & map with possible locations for other pieces

items.name: Treasure Map II
items.note: A peg leg inscribed with a pirate’s treasure map. The map is either false or its treasure has long since been plundered. If you wish to provide the PCs with a true map, then one may be found in the hollow compartment within the leg.

items.name: Trident
items.note: Source: Uberbeard

items.name: Triple Harness
items.note: A leather harness bearing three short quivers (sized for quarrels, rather than arrows). The first holds a dozen red-fletched quarrels, the second a dozen green fletched quarrels, and the third has a dozen white fletched quarrels.

items.name: Troll Horn
items.note: When blown, any trolls within a mile or so will approach the players

items.name: Troll Oboe
items.note: An oboe-like musical instrument made from a troll’s femur.

items.name: Unfinished Zircon Gem
items.note: This gemstone has not yet been finished, but even in its unpolished state it's bright cyan gleam is arresting to the eye. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Unicorn Pipes
items.note: A set of panpipes carved from the bones of a unicorn. When played, they do not make the slightest sound.

items.name: Unspoiled Golden Apples
items.note: A bowl full of golden apples found in the midst of ruins which have long been left desolate and uninhabited. They are quite edible and unspoiled. If they are taken beyond the ruins, they will lose their golden sheen and appear – in all respects – as normal, red apples. So long as they remain within the ruins, however, they are golden, and will not age or rot.

items.name: Unusable Scroll
items.note: A tattered, and sadly unusable, scroll fragment, inscribed with a spell unknown to any living mage.

items.name: Usk Brandy
items.note: A five gallon cask of usk brandy.

items.name: Vial of Antitoxin
items.note: A creature that drinks this vial of liquid gains advantage on saving throws against poison for 1 hour. It confers no benefit to undead or constructs. (Source: PHB)

items.name: Vials of Oil
items.note: Seventy-three clay vials of oil (1 pint each).

items.name: Wand Chamber
items.note: Wand Chamber – fashioned to look like a scroll case, it contains 1d12 slots to hold wands – think of it as a circular bandolier for your everyday wizard

items.name: War Pick
items.note: Source: Uberbeard

items.name: Warhammer
items.note: Source: Uberbeard

items.name: Warning Box
items.note: Box with runic markings for destroy/to be destroyed containing cursed object

items.name: Water Damaged Ledger
items.note: Water damaged ledger – hidden location

items.name: Waterstar Gem
items.note: Waterstar is also called achroite or colourless tourmaline and is rare in the Realms. This stone is riddled with flaws and inclusions and hence only a small portion is fit for cutting. (Source: BGII)

items.name: Weapon Cabinet
items.note: A weapons cabinet, unlocked and immovable, containing: 12 score arrows (1gp/score), 40 javelins (1gp each), 120 crossbow bolts (1sp/each), and a short bow (30gp).

items.name: Whalebone Scrimshaw
items.note: An elaborate, whalebone scrimshaw carving of intertwined orbs, each showing the legend of creation story of one of the seven civilized religions, roughly 10” in diameter.

items.name: Whip
items.note: Source: Uberbeard

items.name: White Ivory Cane
items.note: A cane of the purest white ivory, decorated with ruins unknown to modern scholars, and topped with a handle of polished amber. Trapped within the amber is a strange creature unlike any the PCs have ever seen before.

items.name: White Marble Ball
items.note: A small ball of white marble – roughly three inches across – which hovers in the air until it is touched or disturbed, at which point it falls to the floor and ceases to exhibit any supernatural properties.

items.name: Wooden Wind Chimes
items.note: A set of wooden wind chimes which plays a different tune depending upon the direction of the wind which disturbs it.

items.name: Wrought Iron Sign
items.note: A large, wrought-iron sign crafted to read: “Beware of Wyvern” in a bold script.

items.name: Ziose Gemstone
items.note: Ziose is the name given by sages to a particular facet-grade variety of ziosite; a rare mineral that yields cut stones that flash three vivid hues depending on how the light catches them; purple, blue, and red, or purple, green, and red. Very large (fist- or foot-sized) gems are found and are prized for use in pendants and brooches. (Source: BGII)

items.name: Zircon Gemstone
items.note: Zircon is a brownish crystal found in igneous (volcanic) rocks. Zircon attains its pale blue shade with heating and cutting (usually facet-cut). (Source: SRD)

items.name: Zombie Dust
items.note: A small, brown glass jar, sealed with wax, with a gray dust inside. A white label is affixed to the jar, and reads: “Zombie Dust” in a shaky script. (It is zombie dust.)

items.name: 1d20 Ammunition +1
items.note: You have a bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this piece of magic ammunition. The bonus is determined by the rarity of the ammunition. Once it hit a target, the ammunition is no longer magical. Value listed is for individual units. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Adamantine Armor
items.note: This suit of armor is reinforced with adamantine, one of the hardest substances in existence. While you're wearing it, any critical hit against you becomes a normal hit. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Adventurer's Robe
items.note: This robe has been specially enchanted to meet the eclectic needs of the adventuring mage. Aside from protecting the wearer from various forms of crushing damage, it also provides protection from the basilisk's petrying gaze and polymorphing powers of rival mages. As with other such robes, the Adventurer's Robe can only be worn by those engaged in the wizardly profession. (Source: Baldur's Gate II)

items.name: Aeger's Hide
items.note: This heavy and thick bear hide is all that remains of the fabled Aeger. Several hundred years ago, the Aeger, a giant bear, terrorized hundreds of small villages up and down the Sword Coast. The bear was invulnerable to fire, cold, or acid.
