Finally the young men and women of several villages banded together, forming a militia almost two hundred people strong. The Aeger was surrounded and finally slain. Less than a dozen villagers survived, and they carved the Aeger's hide amongst themselves. Out of one of the pieces this armor was created. (Source: Baldur's Gate II)

items.name: Air Elemental Statue
items.note: This enchanted statuette depicts the swirling form of an air elemental. When stood close, you can feel a cooling breeze swirling around the statuette. (Source: Baldur's Gate II)

items.name: Alchemy Jug
items.note: This ceramic jug appears to be able to hold a gallon of liquid and weighs 12 pounds whether full or empty. Sloshing sounds can be heard from within the jug when it is shaken, even if the jug is empty. You can use an action and name one liquid from the table below to cause the jug to produce the chosen liquid. Afterward, you can uncork the jug as an action and pour that liquid out, up to 2 gallons per minute. The maximum amount of liquid the jug can produce depends on the liquid you named. Once the jug starts producing a liquid, it can't produce a different one, or more of one that has reached its maximum, until the next dawn. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Allmagic Scrolls
items.note: These pieces of unweatherd, thick parchment are lined by a thin string of gold woven into the paper. To anyone who cannot read magic, they are always blank and featureless. However, someone who can read magic realizes that these scrolls can contain spell charges—of ANY type of spell, rather than just Arcane. Paladins, Clerics, and Druids, rejoice! Two charges of the spell must be spent to write one to the scroll, and some scrolls might be found with magic already written to them. These scrolls, once written to, can be cast free of charge—but the scroll is consumed in the process. Store those odd utility spells on Allmagic Scrolls, and never waste an unused daily charge again! (Source: /u/LittleDizzleDaGusha)

items.name: Amber Gemstone
items.note: An amber gemstone of watery gold colouring. It has been polished so that you can see a small petrified leaf suspended inside. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Amethyst Drinking Cup
items.note: An ornate cup fashioned out of a deep purple Amethyst. This material was probably chosen as many believe it to prevent intoxication. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Amulet of Proof Against Detection and Location
items.note: While wearing this amulet, you are hidden from divination magic. You can't be targeted by such magic or perceived through magical scrying sensors. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Ancient Coins
items.note: A cache of ancient coins left from the elder days of the world and made by a civilization utterly alien to the values of today: They are carved from bloodstone, moonstone, and jacinth – with unknown faces and unreadable runes decorating their surface. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Ancient Gold Cube
items.note: Ancient gold cube – quite encumbering. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Ancient Mummy
items.note: An ancient mummy which was given full burial rites and laid within a stately sarcophagus. Arranged on five pedestals around the sarcophagus are the canopic jars in which the mummy’s vital organs were placed. Although the mummy is not of the undead, opening these jars will reveal that its organs continue to function: The heart beats, the lungs fill with air and empty again, and so forth. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Ancient Wand Box
items.note: A collection case holding fifty spent wands – crafted by the finest craftsman and enchanted by the mightiest wizards of a bygone age, but now reduced to mere wood, bone, and glass. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Animal Figurines
items.note: A small box of turquoise animal figurines. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Anthracite Chair
items.note: In this room of the dungeon, there is an elaborate arm chair carved entirely of a single piece of coal. It has been inlaid with silver runes that age has rendered unreadable. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Armageddon Diorama
items.note: A tiny diorama made of oak and silver, depicting a prophecy of the last days of the world in vivid detail. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Armentarious' Scroll Tube
items.note: A very large scroll tube containing the plans for many of the ancient war engines of Armentarious. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Astrologer's Chart
items.note: An astrologer’s chart, inscribed with the finest instruments and the greatest precision upon a vellum which is both supple and expensive. Close inspection by those with the proper knowledge, however, reveals that these charts were constructed around an entirely different night sky – and, possibly, a calendrical system and length of year unlike anything known upon this world. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Aumurille's Chest
items.note: A miniature golden chest with tiny emeralds lining the top that trace out a mage’s sigil. The chest is crafted in exquisite detail. (This is the material component of a “Secret Chest” (from the spell of the same name) for the ruthless lady-mage Aumurille, who will likely want it back....) (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Bag of Holding
items.note: Source: DMG

items.name: Bag of Saffron
items.note: A small bag of saffron. It can be used to flavor the parties travel rations, but is probably more valuable as a trade good. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Bag of Tricks, Grey
items.note: This ordinary gray cloth bag appears to be empty. Reaching inside the bag, however, reveals the presence of a small, fuzzy object. You can use an action to pull the fuzzy object from the bag and throw it up to 20 feet. When the object lands, it transforms into a creature you determine by rolling a d8 and consulting the table that corresponds to the bag’s color. The creature vanishes at the next dawn or when it is reduced to 0 hit points. The creature is friendly to you and your companions, and it acts on your turn. You can use a bonus action to command how the creature moves and what action it takes on its next turn, or to give it general orders, such as to attack your enemies. In the absence of such orders, the creature acts in a fashion appropriate to its nature. Once three fuzzy objects have been pulled from the bag, the bag cannot be used again until the next dawn. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Bagpipe of Invisibility
items.note: A wind instrument consisting of a reed melody pipe and from one to five drones with air supplied continuously either by a bag with valve-stopped mouth tube or by bellows. The bag itself is patterned in red and blue. (Source: /u/dustybullets)

items.name: Bat Sack
items.note: Witch’s Mad Sack of Bats – releases swarm of bats (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Battle Banner
items.note: A famous battle banner, long lost, smells badly (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Bear Claw Boots
items.note: A pair of dark leather boots upon which a curious gnomish mechanism has been fastened around. There is a small lever on the side of the ankle that, when toggled, extends on each boot a set of sharp mountaineering claws. The boots are sized for medium humanoid but the mechanism itself would be easy enough for a blacksmith to scale up or down. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Behir Horn
items.note: A stoppered behir horn containing 300 gp worth of powdered electrum (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Belt of the Shippey
items.note: A dark, water-worn leather belt embossed with an intricate seascape all around its girth. It has a polished golden buckle, as well as the name “Jadmek” carved into the front. It confers a +2 bonus to all Use Rope checks, as well as a +2 bonus to knowledge of anything maritime. Any encounter with pirates or seafaring merchants might prove interesting if the NPCs spot this belt. (Source: /u/LittleDizzleDaGusha)

items.name: Bird Cage
items.note: An antique golden bird cage with an electrum filigree. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Black Lotus Pollen
items.note: Four half-pound bricks of compressed Black Lotus pollen (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Black Mountain Stout Recipe
items.note: A 1’ x 3’ bronze plate bearing the ancient, and secret recipe for Black Mountain Stout (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Blank Contract
items.note: A contract with blank parts. Can be modified into any sort of deal you wish. The first party to break the contract is blasted with a beam from above (d12, ignore armour) and the contract dissolves into shredded parchment.

items.name: Blood Amulet
items.note: A bronze circle held by a red ribbon.

items.name: Blood Waterskin
items.note: Waterskin of lamb’s blood

items.name: Blue Dragon Figurine
items.note: A finely crafted figurine of a blue dragon, rampant, done in wood and painted.

items.name: Boomerang of Retrieval
items.note: This otherwise normal looking boomerang has a sense of arcana deep within it's dark oak core. Researching it's history will reveal that this is, in fact, one of many created by a local wizard who was frustrated at being unable to throw a normal boomerang in such a way that it returned naturally. (Source: D&D Wiki)

items.name: Boots of Elvenkind
items.note: While you wear these boots, your steps make no sound, regardless of the surface you are moving across. You also have advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks that rely on moving silently. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Boots of Striding and Springing
items.note: A pair of tan boots which are fastened around the foot with three adjustable belts. The inner sole of these boots feels springy to the touch and seems to mould itself around the user's foot for a fit so comfortable it's like walking on the clouds. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Boots of the Winterlands
items.note: A pair of hide boots lined with dense, warm white fur. The eyelet for each bootlace is patterned with small gemstones and the sole of the boot is spiked so as to benefit the user over icy and unstable terrain. These furred boots are snug and feel quite warm. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Box o' Diseases
items.note: An ornate box containing corked vials of different diseases

items.name: Box of Evil Miniatures
items.note: A collection of humanoid figurines, each poised in an unspeakable act of evil. They are packed in box which has been wrapped in lamb’s wool.

items.name: Bracers of Archery
items.note: While wearing these bracers, you have proficiency with the longbow and shortbow, and you gain a +2 bonus to damage rolls on ranged attacks made with such weapons. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Bracers of Binding
items.note: The bracers appear to be Bracers of Specialization, but when donned, bind the wearer wrists together, making it quite difficult to wield a weapon or cast a spell. These bracers are sometimes offered as rewards for services rendered by the Drow. The recipient, believing them to be very powerful, will often don them before examinating them. At this point, the drow find it amusing to conjure a hook horror. (Source: BGII)

items.name: Brass Dragon Egg
items.note: A delicate brass dragon egg; hollowed out and painted into a seascape, resting on a wooden stand.

items.name: Brass Mug
items.note: A plain brass mug with a surprisingly ornate jade inlay. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Bronze Caltrops
items.note: A cask full of bronze caltrops.

items.name: Bronze Crown
items.note: An old bronze crown, once inlaid with gemstones. They have since been picked out, but the crown itself is wonderfully cast. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Bronze Tower Shield
items.note: A tower shield of bronze, cast into the likeness of a chimera’s heads and enameled to provide the appropriate colors. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Brooch of Shielding
items.note: While wearing this brooch, you have resistance to force damage, and you have immunity to damage from the magic missile spell. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Broom of Flying
items.note: This wooden broom functions like a mundane broom until you stand astride it and speak its command word. It then hovers beneath you and can be ridden in the air. The broom stops hovering when you land. You can send the broom to travel alone to a destination within 1 mile of you if you speak the command word, name the location, and are familiar with that place. The broom comes back to you when you speak another command word, provided that the broom is still within 1 mile of you. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Bull's Head Ring
items.note: A silver ring shaped into the likeness of a bull’s head.

items.name: Bullette Horn Barding
items.note: A complete barding for a war horse made of Bullette horn plates.

items.name: Cap of Water Breathing
items.note: The word “STYX” is sewn into the inner headband of this cap. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Ceramic Jars
items.note: 4 ceramic canopic jars, holding the stomach, intestines, lungs, and liver of a long-ago monarch. Each has a golden lid with a likeness of a paunchy, hook-nosed man with pale skin and a squint.

items.name: Chess Set
items.note: A large, boxed chess set. All the pieces are small, dead pixies somehow sealed within glass.

items.name: Chrysoberyl Gem
items.note: Chrysoberyl is a hard, transparent, green gem which is usually facet-cut. It is said that Chrysoberyl has ties to the outer planes and hence its use with battling demonic possession and the undead. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Clàrsach Harp
items.note: A clàrsach harp in the style of the northern barbarians, but crafted with an elegance which suggests the decadence of a great civilization. Its strings are almost too fine to be perceived with the naked eye, and when they are played they resonate with the force of a full orchestra.

items.name: Cloak of Partial Shadow
items.note: A normal looking black cloak with a high collar. When activated it gives off small puffs of black smoke that help obscure the wearer. (Source: AdamLapka)

items.name: Companion Cube
items.note: A Companion Cube: It was created by a wizard who had stuck himself in a tower to show complete dedication to his studies. However, after several decades he became so lonely that he chose to make a friend... literally. Using what little non-component materials he had laying around his tower, he created a small cube and gave it sentience. It was able to communicate telepathically with anyone who made a link with the cube. Unfortunately, the wizard eventually died and the cube became lonely. Unable to travel and find a new friend, the cube simply sat there and waited for anyone to come along. Anyone seeing the cube would be compelled to be with the cube and have it in their possession. Upon holding the cube, they would feel the need to keep it with them at all times, and protect it as if it were their own child. Little by little, the connection between the cube and it's possessor would grow, until the two would be inseparable. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Coral Circlet
items.note: A dark crimson Coral gemstone fitted into a golden circlet. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Crayon Torches
items.note: This set of 12 torches burn different colors. There are two sets of Red, Yellow, Blue, Green, Orange and Purple torches.

items.name: Crystal Sword
items.note: When used this sword will deliver 1 instant hit for max damage, including bonuses. Once used, the sword shatters into crystal shards. Anyone within one square must roll reflex or take 1d4 damage from shard pieces.

items.name: Dagger of Slicing
items.note: Dagger of slicing – ignores armor bonus

items.name: Death Talker Charm
items.note: Charm that tells 1 truth about a creature killed in the area

items.name: Debt of a Soul
items.note: Debt of a Soul – contract of soul ownership (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Deck of Cards
items.note: An ancient deck of cards with five suits – clovers, swords, clubs, hearts, and dragons.

items.name: Decrepit Skeleton in Armor
items.note: The skeletal remains of a long-dead warrior, wearing a suit of plate armor which has long since tarnished. The name of every warrior to ever die by the last wearer’s hand has been inscribed upon the front and back of the chest plate, covering nearly every inch of its surface with chaotically written, black runes.

items.name: Devil Salts
items.note: Awakens any creature from sleep or unconsciousness, but they flail and scream in terror for a few seconds first. They have no memory of this afterwards.

items.name: Dragon Balm
items.note: Dragon balm – reduced heat damage (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Dragon Statuette
items.note: A statuette of a dragon, forged from the finest mithril, which responds to yes or no questions which are posed to it in draconic. The answers given may appear prophetic or oracular, but are actually random.

items.name: Druid's Magnet
items.note: A wood chip which acts as a magnet, despite its non-metallic nature.

items.name: Drums of Endurance
items.note: Once per day, give +10ft to movement, and +1 to attack and damage rolls to all allies.

items.name: Dryad Bomb
items.note: A so-called "Dryad Bomb", called such because when thrown it creates a mess of magically grown tangles and vines. Appears to be a glowing orange orb surrounded by a series of vines and roots. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Duergar Compass
items.note: This compass is carved out of heavy, hard Petrebony (a wood that grows in caves). A brass needle sits in the center. However, this compass' needle points East, not North. There are no markings on its surface. (Source: /u/LittleDizzleDaGusha)

items.name: Dust of Appearance
items.note: Stack of 4, user covers 20ft radius in magic dust that shows anything that is hidden or dispellable.

items.name: Dust of Coughing and Sneezing
items.note: Magical dust that makes anyone cough and sneeze (combat)

items.name: Dust of Dispelling
items.note: stack of 4, user covers 20ft radius in magic dust that shows anything that is hidden or dispellable.

items.name: Dwarven Bomb
items.note: Source: Uberbeard

items.name: Edible Greatsword
items.note: Works like a normal greatsword. Great tasting and low in calories! Regenerates 6 inches per day. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Eldritch Flint & Steel
items.note: Eldritch flint & steel – 10’ reach – blinding/minor burns

items.name: Elven Bomb
items.note: An orange-yellow shell with small claw-like protrusions. This appears more to have been grown rather than constructed. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Elven Crescent Blade
items.note: A striking weapon defined by its deeply curved, moon-shaped blade and favoured by Drow. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Elven Engraved Combs
items.note: An elegant carved comb of oak, engraved with the name Aethelren and decorated with twin dragons. Elsewhere, a similar comb of ash, engraved with the name Ilthorien and decorated with twin unicorns, can be found.

items.name: Empty Mason Jar
items.note: This magical mason jar is made of uneven, lumpy, blue-green glass with a copper lid. No matter what is placed in this transparent jar, it will always appear to be empty from the outside--so long as the lid is on. On the lid is pressed “calasse,” Elven for “clean.” It cannot hold more than its actual volume. (Source: /u/LittleDizzleDaGusha)

items.name: Enchanted Apple
items.note: A purple-colored apple. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Exploding Gold Coins
items.note: Exploding gold coins (Looks legit. Should avoid contact with real gold coins for safety...)

items.name: Exploding Rations
items.note: Exploding rations – breathe weapon 1d6 hours for 1 use of color theme type

items.name: F**k You Fireworks
items.note: These appear to be normal Gnomish fireworks (although even those might seem unique and valuable to anyone unfamiliar with Gnomish ingenuity) but when they are eventually set off they spell out giant obscenities across the night's sky. It seems, in retrospect, that they may have been created by a rather angry Gnome. Sadly, whomever created the fireworks and who they were originally intended for will forever remain a mystery. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Finely Woven Rug
items.note: A finely made 10’ x 12’ wool floor rug. The center of the rug depicts a scene of siege warfare. A Halfling is being flung over a city wall by a trebuchet. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Flask of Nightmare Ale
items.note: Half-full flask of nightmare ale – 1 sip = 1d4 hours fear check per hour; full measure = 1d4 hours horrifying hallucinations vs DC save for early retirement

items.name: Flask of St. Cuthbert
items.note: A pair of steel flasks, sealed with wax bearing the symbol of St. Cuthbert. (Holy water, 25 gp each)

items.name: Fundial
items.note: A sundial which runs backwards.

items.name: Garnet Gemstone
items.note: Garnet is a general class of crystals ranging from deep red to violet in colour. a typical cut for this rare isometric gem is 12 to 24 sides, with the very rare 36 sides being known from time to time. it is rumored a 48-sided garnet exists although none have ever been able to track down its location. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Giant Boomerang
items.note: This massive boomerang requires two hands by medium sized humanoids. This may have been specially designed for an ogre, or giant. It has sharp metal blades that run down the side of each wing. The blades on the inner curves make it a bit tricky to wield. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Giant-King's Necklace
items.note: Giant-King’s platinum necklace with crimson diamond worn by dragon

items.name: Giant's Arrow
items.note: A huge arrow, with a steel tip and copper wire inlaid spirally down the shaft. To a medium or large creature, it behaves just like a javelin. However, when thrown, it whistles loudly. (Source: /u/LittleDizzleDaGusha)

items.name: Glaive
items.note: A three bladed disk which can either be used in melee or as a thrown weapon. The circular handle is made from dark wood and has the design of an owl's head in the middle. (Source: D&D Wiki)

items.name: Glaive
items.note: This is a short pole-arm resembling a Fauchard or Guan do, with an additional spike for thrusting. It deals slashing damage when wielded in one hand and piercing damage when wielded in two hands. (Source: D&D Wiki)

items.name: Globe of Invulnerability
items.note: Globe of Invulnerability (It's a snowglobe. Not even the gods themselves can destroy it.)

items.name: Gloves of Slapping
items.note: Source: Unknown

items.name: Glowing Longsword
items.note: A longsword made from a single piece of a dull, almost glossy, metal. It feels slightly heavier than expected and the handle is always warm to the touch. (Source: Lamp_05)

items.name: Glowing Shortsword
items.note: A shortsword made from a single piece of a dull, almost glossy, metal. It feels slightly heavier than expected and the handle is always warm to the touch. (Source: Lamp_05)

items.name: Gnome Bomb
items.note: An ornate, egg-shaped device made with cast bronze. There is a pin in the top of the device with an etched note indicating the following: PULL THYS PINNE AND WAITE TENNE COUNTS BEFORRE BOOME. When the pin is pulled, an urgent ticking sound begins to eminate from the device. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Gnome Camo Paint
items.note: This paint provides no protection, but grants 10 to disguise. The only way it works, however, is if the wearer is wearing no clothes, and covered head to toe in the stuff. It's hilarious when a disguise check fails, and they realize they are staring at a naked Gnome.

items.name: Gnome Timepiece
items.note: A magical time-keeping device produced by a Gnomish artisan. Opening this circular locket allows you to check the current time based on where a delicate hand is pointing. On one half of the circular face is an ornate night's sky with a crescent moon indicating midnight. On the other half is a blue sky, with a bright sun indicating mid-day. It's unclear how the magic has been infused with the device, as no arcane presence is detectable, but pulling the silver panel off the back of the locket simply exposes a number of delicate, ticking gears. Gnome magic is compicated. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: God Ring
items.note: Ring of a god

items.name: Gold Bracelet
items.note: A golden bracelet. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Gold Ring
items.note: A small gold ring set with bloodstones. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Golden Arrows
items.note: A quiver of golden arrows, They are devoid of supernatural properties, but despite their unusual composition will perform as normal arrows would.

items.name: Golden Tablet
items.note: A tablet of pure gold, inscribed with the core rites and beliefs of a venerable religion. Careful study of this tablet, however, will reveal subtle – but important – differences between these ancient practices and the current practices of the religion in question.

items.name: Great Axe
items.note: A hardwood handle topped with a heavy doublesided axe blade. It's construction is immaculate, and it's design is so archetypal you can't help but think, "Wow, what a great axe." (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Greataxe of Blindness
items.note: This is a large greataxe, with a steel shaft and silver floating blade much like the Telescoping Glaive. (Source: /u/LittleDizzleDaGusha)

items.name: Greedy Bag of Holding
items.note: This appears to be a common cloth sack about 2 feet by 4 feet in size. (Source: William Orfanakos)

items.name: Griffon Bomb
items.note: A metallic egg-shaped weapon which, when thrown, sprouts magical wings and hovers above it's intended target. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Heart Shaped Token
items.note: A small, heart-shaped token carved out of rose-colored sandstone, holding the mad soul of a princess who was trapped there by a sorcerer many centuries ago. Her body remains in the hidden royal sepulchers of her homeland, perfectly preserved by ancient magic.

items.name: Helm of Comprehending Languages
items.note: A fully enclosed helmet of matte grey metal with a small chainmail nape covering. Rather than a simple gap, two green gems have been embedded to give an eye-like appearance, and as additional flourish a set of elven ears have been moulded into each side. (Source: SRD)

items.name: Hero Dolls
items.note: These start getting made after the party does something particularly heroic. Little cloth dolls that look like party members, when squeezed, the caster spouts illusionary flames, the cleric spouts prayers "Praise Avandra!", the fighter has kung-fu action where he swings his sword.

items.name: Hidden Blade Bracer
items.note: Concealed punching dagger-bracer

items.name: Holy Ice Symbol
items.note: An ornate block of ice carved into an ancient holy symbol. The carving has a subtle taint of magic to it, but it's not exactly clear what prevents it from melting. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Horn Coral Gem
items.note: Horn Coral, which is also called night coral, is a deep black coral, similar to angel's skin save for its solid color. It is incredibly difficult to work with, and is used in jewelry as a polished twig or branch of material, or is cut cabochon. (Source: BGII)

items.name: Immovable Rod
items.note: This flat iron rod has a button on one end. You can use an action to press the button, which causes the rod to become magically fixed in place. Until you or another creature uses an action to push the button again, the rod doesn't move, even if it is defying gravity. The rod can hold up to 8,000 pounds of weight. More weight causes the rod to deactivate and fall. A creature can use an action to make a DC 30 Strength check, moving the fixed rod up to 10 feet on a success.

items.name: Ivory Statuette
items.note: A carved sculpture of jungle animals on an ivory tusk. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Jade Buttons
items.note: A small collection of antique Jade buttons. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Jade Statue
items.note: A Weather-worn jade statue depicting a desert beast known only to the people of the far south.

items.name: Jade Telescope
items.note: A telescope of jade and banded with bloodstone. A character looking through the telescope sees not the world around them, but a strange, alien panorama which – nonetheless – follows the motions of the telescope.

items.name: Jet Brooch
items.note: A deep black brooch set with a Jet gemstone. Rumor suggests a brooch of this nature helps prevent the evil eye. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Jewel Encrusted Sarcophagus
items.note: Jewel encrusted sarcophagus – mummy inside

items.name: Kazoo of Intimidation
items.note: When this Kazoo is played, it grants +2 to Intimidation checks. A +3 if the player has a kazoo in the room.

items.name: Large Snow Globe
items.note: A large snow globe, with a study base of polished oak, depicting a small farmhouse in the midst of a forest clearing. Shaking the globe causes the snow to gently fall, and as it does miniature figures exit the house and begin performing chores about the farmyard. The figures return to the house as the last flake falls.

items.name: Lava Rocks
items.note: Rocks stuffed with lava

items.name: Lance
items.note: Source: PHB

items.name: Learning Languages by Chompsky
items.note: A book detailing the general process of learning a language, from both a practical and theoretical angle. Written by a talented linguist called Chompsky the Gnome. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Leomund's Loot Rack
items.note: This is a rack of weapons, armor, books, wands, whatever you desire. It holds anywhere from 3-6 items, each usually magical in nature. Each can be examined visually and by a Detect Magic spell. However, as soon as one is touched, the others disappear—never to be found again. Multiple items cannot be removed at once. Optionally a password could dispel the magic, allowing all items to be removed. (Source: /u/LittleDizzleDaGusha)

items.name: Longsword of Balanced Sums
items.note: The Longsword of Balanced Sums. Functions exactly like a generic +2 Longsword... except when you crit miss. Then it heals the target for half its max hit points.

items.name: Lucky Coin
items.note: You must have this item with you for 24 hours for it to become attuned to you. Once attuned, you may choose the result any time it is flipped, regardless of who actually flips it. Possible commands include heads, tails, and edge.

items.name: Lucky Dodge Belt
items.note: Once an encounter, the player can choose to roll a d20 and use that as their base AC instead of 10. Player must take the value of the roll.

items.name: Mace of Chance
items.note: A simple wooden handle with a large stone macehead that has been shaped into a icosahedron. It is a somewhat unweildy weapon due to it's immense weight, but you would not wish to be the recipient of a solid blow. On each of it's twenty sides you can make out faded engravings, anyone who can read Celestial will identify these as numbers. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Magic Litmus Paper
items.note: Litmus magic paper: This paper can be used as a standard action by rubbing it on an object. It either detects no magic and can be used again, or changed color and brightness to reflect the school(s) and strengths of magic on the object.

items.name: Meal Table
items.note: A stone table in the midst of the wilderness which is, nevertheless, always filled with a fresh meal whenever travelers come across it.

items.name: Medallion of Courage
items.note: Once per day, swift. remove AC of target granted by armor, last one turn. user takes penalty to AC equal to target for same duration.

items.name: Menesta's Book of Stored Skill
items.note: This magical book has no discernable writings on the outside, or on any page. As soon as it is opened up, ask the player for the first three skills that come to mind. As soon as they tell you, the first page of the book becomes a table of contents with three chapters. The names of the chapters are the three skills they mentioned. Turning to the page of a skill chapter causes the book to disappear in a colored burst of light—and leaves the user with a permanent +1 rank to said skill. If their ranks are maxed out, the book is wasted. (Source: /u/LittleDizzleDaGusha)

items.name: Merchant's Dagger Box
items.note: A merchant’s leather case containing a selection of twenty well-made daggers of varying designs.

items.name: Minting Tools
items.note: A set of dies and hammers for minting the king's coinage.

items.name: Mithral Breastplate
items.note: This armor consists of a fitted mithral chest piece worn with supple leather. Although it leaves the legs and arms relatively unprotected, this armor provides good protection for the wearer's vital organs while leaving the wearer relatively unencumbered. (Source: PHB)

items.name: Mithral Chain Mail Armor
items.note: Made of interlocking metal rings, chain mail includes a layer of quilted fabric worn underneath the mail to prevent chafing and to cushion the impact of blows. The suit includes gauntlets. (Source: PHB)

items.name: Mithral Chain Shirt
items.note: Made of interlocking metal rings, a chain shirt is worn between layers of clothing or leather. This armor offers modest protection to the wearer's upper body and allows the sound of the rings rubbing against one another to be muffled by outer layers. (Source: PHB)

items.name: Mithral Half Plate Armor
items.note: Shaped mithral plates that cover most of the wearer's body. It does not include leg protection beyond simple greaves that are attached with leather straps. (Source: PHB)

items.name: Mithral Plate Armor
items.note: Plate consists of shaped, interlocking metal plates to cover the entire body. A suit of plate includes gauntlets, heavy leather boots, a visored helmet, and thick layers of padding underneath the armor. Buckles and straps distribute the weight over the body. (Source: PHB)

items.name: Mithral Ring Mail Armor
items.note: This armor is leather armor with heavy rings sewn into it. The rings help reinforce the armor against blows from swords and axes. Ring mail is inferior to chain mail, and it's usually worn only by those who can't afford better armor. (Source: PHB)

items.name: Mithral Scale Mail Armor
items.note: This armor consists of a coat and leggings of leather covered with overlapping pieces of mithral, much like the scales of a fish. The suit includes gauntlets. (Source: PHB)

items.name: Mithral Splint Armor
items.note: This armor is made of narrow vertical strips of metal riveted to a backing of leather that is worn over cloth padding. Flexible chain mail protects the joints. (Source: PHB)

items.name: Moonbar Gemstone
items.note: Moonbar is a pearly white, opaque gemstone, usually pale blue with green and gold mottling. Related in type to fire and black opals, but is only slightly more common. (Source: BGII)

items.name: Moss Earplugs
items.note: Two peanut-sized balls of moss sit atop gnarled wooden knobs. Placing one of them in each ear causes total deafness—or total hearing protection, whichever your preference. The player cannot hear anything, and must succeed on a DC 16 Will save to speak a single sentence comprehensibly. However, they are totally immune to all sonic attacks and songs and can fall asleep extremely well. The earplugs can be removed at will. (Source: /u/LittleDizzleDaGusha)

items.name: Neverfull Waterskin
items.note: Neverfull Waterskin (It's always half empty.)

items.name: Obsidian Dolphin
items.note: An obsidian figurine of a dolphin which is always wet to the touch.

items.name: Old Guard's Note
items.note: Within a rotten pouch found upon the body of a long dead soldier is a parchment with an unbroken seal. If the seal is broken and the message read, it warns its would-be recipient to guard against an eminent betrayal.

items.name: Orkon's Club
items.note: An inscription in the smooth handle of this simple spiked mace indicates that it was once wielded by a diminuative Gnome warrior named Orkon. (Source: Fable)

items.name: Ornate Abacus
items.note: An abacus with counting beads made of ivory, silver, gold, and vitrified eye balls. (Source: Unknown)

items.name: Ornate Rapier
items.note: A rapier in an ornate, electrum-trimmed steel scabbard worked in swirl-patterns.

items.name: Pearl
items.note: Pearls consist of layers of aragonite agonizingly formed around a bit of grit or other irritant inside oysters and other mollusks. The resulting pearl has a rich, deep luster. Most of the pearls in the realms are white, however there are varieties that are much more rare such as rainbow and black. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Porcelain Discs
items.note: One hundred and sixteen 3” white porcelain discs, each has a 1 ounce knob of electrum embedded in its center

items.name: Potion of False Bluff
items.note: A potion vial containing a sweet. honey-smelling golden syrup. There is a label on the vial written in common, which reads "Potion of Deception". (Source: Unknown)

items.name: Potion of Harmless Flaying
items.note: A pearlescent green potion in a glass vial that seems to swirl playfully when started at. (Source: Sherwood Bosco)

items.name: Pouch of Healing Potions
items.note: A large carry pouch made from the skin of a monkey. Inside are 1d6 Potions of Greater Healing. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Pouch of Mushrooms
items.note: A small brown pouch that smells faintly musty. Inside it are 1d4 colourful mushrooms that are unrecognizable, even to a particularly knowledgeable alchemist or naturist. If determined, a painstaking delve through a herbalists library might identify the mushrooms as "sub-space mushrooms" in a madly written diary of a man claiming to be a deposed king called Cooper. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Puppet Theater
items.note: An elegant puppet theater. Although its wood is brightly polished and the red of its satin curtains is bright and fresh, the hand-painted sign upon its front (reading “The Wondrous Show of Punch and Judy” in carnival lettering) is chipped and faded. If the curtains are opened (using a set of finely woven pulley ropes which lie inside the theater), the puppets – stored in a concealed compartment “backstage” – will come to life and perform elaborate shows of their own volition.

items.name: Quicksilver Surprise
items.note: In a worn and decaying wall of mud bricks, a single black brick – polished and perfectly preserved – stands out in stark contrast. If the brick is pried out and broken open, it will reveal a quicksilver core.

items.name: Rabbit's Foot
items.note: A lucky rabbit’s foot which hangs from a golden chain. Although separated from the rest of the rabbit, the foot magically lives on: It will respond to touch, bleed if injured, and so forth.

items.name: Raven Feather Fans
items.note: A pair of pole mounted fans made of giant raven feathers

items.name: Raven Pen
items.note: A pen made from a raven’s feather. Through some magical means it writes continuously from an inexhaustible supply of golden ink upon whatever piece of parchment may be near by.

items.name: Ricochet Sling
items.note: Ricochet-sling – up to 4 targets (d4 for targets, attack roll each; first attack fail causes ricochet to end [unless a tie, in the case of a tie to AC the ricochet continues])

items.name: Ring of Dubious Might
items.note: A silver ring, encrusted with a small topaz. Upon wearing it on their finger, the wearer feels incredibly strong and confident. However, entering combat, their confidence immediately dissipates, leaving them terrified. (Source: Jacos)

items.name: Ring of Fake Invisibility
items.note: A silver ring inlaid with a trasluscent pearl. There is an inscription in Elvish which reads, "To Elabella, perhaps now you will find your mirror less engrossing."

items.name: Ring of Good Luck
items.note: This ring imbues the wearer +1 on all saves.

items.name: Romeot and Julio
items.note: One of the most famous plays in the works of the famous Orcish bard "Villam Spearshaker". It tells the story of two starcrossed lovers whose familes are locked in a bitter rivalry. They attempt to run away together only to end up eaten by an ogre. The ogre then proceeds to eat most of the two feuding families, all of the side characters, and in a number of early renditions of the play a few members of the audience as well. A note to budding directors: Try not to cast an actual ogre as the ogre. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Romeot and Julio II
items.note: A sequel to the original play by the famous Orcish bard; Villam Speakshaker. Romeot and Julio (or at least, what's left of them) are ressurected as shambling zombies in service to a wealthy merchant. The play ends with the merchant attempting to extract a pound of flesh from Romeot to pay for a debt before getting eaten alive by the two zombies. Their final moments of the play are spent lovingly feeding each other his bloody entrails. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Salve of Silence
items.note: Salve of Silence – armor improving sound nullifier

items.name: Screaming Arrows
items.note: A quarry of 20 Screaming Arrows – intimidate/fear check

items.name: Sentient Dagger of Sneaking
items.note: This sentient dagger attempts to help anytime it's owner tries to sneak by whistling inconspicuously.  For some reason it seems to have been discarded. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Shell Paste Lotion
items.note: A small metal tin which has been carefully labelled. Instructions are included on the inside of the lid, "Smear on bare skin to grow a natural defence." The paste inside is white and gritty. It smells like coconut. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Shrunken Head
items.note: Shrunken head with command word – 1 ray spell per hour

items.name: Signet Ring of Gold
items.note: A large signet ring of gold, worked with a unicorn crest. Any commoner who dons the ring will suddenly discover its metal heated to unbearable levels – causing one hit point of damage per round. However, anyone with noble blood (however slight), or destined to become a noble at some point in the future, who does the same will suffer only a momentary flash of pain (with an accompanying 1 hp of damage). They will find that the unicorn crest has reshaped itself to their own heraldry, which has also been branded in miniature form upon the back of the finger. From this point forward, they can put on or remove the ring at will without any further ill effects.

items.name: Silk Robe
items.note: A long silk robe with golden embroidery. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Silver Necklace
items.note: This silver necklace has a gemstone pendant. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Skull Idol
items.note: Gold dipped idol of several skulls

items.name: Smoke of Deceit
items.note: One time use only, grants perfect hide and move silently rolls to all allies. effect is lost if moved within 10ft of enemy

items.name: Snakeskin Scimitar
items.note: A scimitar with a gold and snakeskin hilt set with a citrine. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Solomon's Fishing Pole
items.note: A cursed fishing pole made of blackened ash. It will never catch a fish – although, if one attempts to use it unbaited, they will succeed in catching skeleton fish.

items.name: Sortida Compass
items.note: Brass dungeon compass, runic markings, always points to nearest exit.

items.name: Sphene Gem
items.note: Sphene is a soft, brittle gemstone (and, like scapra, easily worked by unskilled cutters) of various yellow to green shades, the most prized of these being a fine emerald green. (Source: BGII)

items.name: Spider-Silk Rope
items.note: Giant spider silk rope – sticks to most surfaces (including oiled gloves)

items.name: Spinel Necklace
items.note: A spun leather necklace of deep green Spinel beads. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Staff of Eulosus
items.note: A slender staff of pale, living wood, that is unexpectedly light . Its surface is etched with spiralling runes that resemble rising currents of air, and when held, the engravings give off a faint, silvery glow—like moonlight caught in motion. (Source: Aziraphale)

items.name: Staff of Force
items.note: once per day, standard. push target 10 ft the direction they are facing. once per day. If target is pushed into another creature, they deal 1d20 damage. to each other. If target is pushed into object, they take 1d20. Target can make a fort save against the push, goes against users 1d20+will mod

items.name: Stem-Stone
items.note: Pebble that transforms into whatever inorganic substance it last touched. Currently the leather of your pouch.

items.name: Storm Giant Coins
items.note: Thirty storm giant minted gold coins, each is the size of a dinner plate and weighs 17 lbs. The image stuck on the coins shows a giant hand flicking a human off a table.

items.name: Storm Giant Horn
items.note: A horn fashioned from the translucent bone of a storm giant. Those who put their ear to the horn hear it whisper the sounds of an unknown village square. Whether the village which they hear is – or was – a real place is a mystery which may never be solved. (Twin horn in village square allows communication between the two).

items.name: Sword of Battle Fury
items.note: Battle Fury-grants the feat Cleave

items.name: Talking Toad
items.note: Enchanted talking toad – polymorphed noble – knowledge of multiple regions, religions & other worldly strangeness

items.name: Tapestry
items.note: A large, well-made tapestry depicting a local duke. (Source: DMG)

items.name: The Bloody Burden
items.note: Each day this blade draws no blood it becomes heavier to bear. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: The Elven Mirror
items.note: A plain mirror with a frame of pale ashwood. Whenever someone looks in the mirror, however, they perceive an elven face in place of their own.

items.name: The Erian Tapestry
items.note: A famous (or forgotten) tapestry depicting the Fall of Erian. The figures upon the tapestry move and change, re-enacting – in an artistically abstracted form – the legendary battle over the course of half an hour.

items.name: The Guardsman's Friend
items.note: A simple dagger with what seems to be a blood stain on the blade that cannot be washed away. On very close examination, if the leather wrapping of the handle is peeled back the phrase "All Knights are Bad" has been written on the tang. (Source: OneManBland)

items.name: The Headless Helm
items.note: A frog-mouth helmet with a distinctly copper hue, though it a composite metal of some kind. The visor has a jagged curve and brings to mind the wicked smile of a Jack O'Lantern. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: The Living Lantern
items.note: An an ordinary candle lamp with a glass surround and handle. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: The Plaster Sword
items.note: The legendary sword once owned by the Goblin hero "Stink", who was also known as the "Hero of Thyme" after he slew a greedy Human merchant and stole a wagon of expensive cooking spices. The sword itself appears to be ornate and magical in nature from afar, but when handled it is clear that it is simply a clever replica made out of gypsum and mud that has been painted. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: The Rhinoclean Helm
items.note: A large iron cap with visor shaped like the head of a rhinoceros beetle. (Source: Scribnibbler21)

items.name: The Scalpel
items.note: A surgeons scalpel, tempered so that it is both stronger and sharper than a normal blade. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: The Torturer's Dagger
items.note: Found on the corpse of an enemy from the realm of chaos. (Source: Unknown)

items.name: Throwing Axe of Appendage Severing
items.note: If the user of this throwing axe manages a critical hit, it will sever an appendage. – critical = appendage severed

items.name: Thunder Pommel
items.note: When embedded in the hilt of a weapon. -deal and additional 1d6 lightning damage on attacks with an odd 1d20 roll

items.name: Tiny Phantasm
items.note: A floating mass of swirling colours the size of a penny. It follows your every command and leaves a multicolour trail across any surface it touches. Cannot physically interact in any other way.

items.name: Totem Pole
items.note: A pinewood totem pole of the northern barbarians, bearing six faces (from top to bottom): a hawk, a demon, an elk, a man, a fox, and a white dragon. The hawk has a silver feather on the left side of its face (8gp), the demon has a gold nose-ring (6gp), the elk has copper antlers (4gp), the man has electrum eyes (3gp each), the fox has a black onyx nose (15gp), and the dragon has ivory teeth (30 teeth worth 1gp each). The pole is fifteen feet tall and weighs 200 pounds. (4.57m, 90.7kg)

items.name: Tourmaline Gemstone
items.note: A multi colored rectangular Tourmaline gemstone. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Townshend's Cudgel
items.note:
Townshend's Crudgels was invented by its namesake, Peter "Unknown" Townshend, a traveling bard who wished his lute to both give beauty and take it away (preferably with a good old fashioned thrashing). His lute design, later copied by some of his admirers, was a simple oak lute, reinforced with an iron shell, to maintain the soft vibration of the sound against the wooden interior, and to give the lute the strength to crack open an orc's skull with a well placed blow.
This weapon functions both as a lute and a weapon. When used as a lute, it is considered to be a lute and does not require a separate proficiency bonus to play. However, if the user rolls a critical miss with the weapon (a 1 on an attack roll) the lute may become slightly damaged and will require the user to spend an hour to retune it. If the instrument is not returned, whenever the instrument is played, the user is disadvantaged on the Perform check. This weapon can also be used as a bard's spellcasting focus. (Source: D&D Wiki)

items.name: Trap Detection Key
items.note: A token/key designed to deactivate all traps within the dungeon, requires activation at security box in secret depths of deepest part of dungeon

items.name: Triple Barreled Crossbow
items.note: Triple ‘barreled’ crossbow

items.name: Troglodyte Egg
items.note: A brown and red flecked egg, about the size of a loaf of bread, that gives off a rancid scent.

items.name: Troll-Rubber Boots
items.note: Troll-rubber boots. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Underworld Currency
items.note: Underworld Currency – lead coins; purple rocks/crystals, fairy dust, dragon scales, dna globules (rocks/capsules with runic understanding of specific creatures), tally bones, soul traps/crystals

items.name: Visikus
items.note: This dagger belonged to the great druid, Marcek, a wise and cunning elf who fell only to the fiery Wyrm, Brazzemel, during his quest to reclaim the lost Eladrin spell tomes. Marcek inherited this dagger from his master and mentor, Garbasen the Sage, who crafted it with ancient Eladrin magic. Covered in etched runes and jewels, this dagger holds a legendary history and a powerful magic core.

items.name: Wand of Eye-Poking
items.note: A wand of eye poking, four charges. It will unerringly poke out the eye of a target creature within arm’s reach.

items.name: War Scythe
items.note: Fashioned to resemble the threshing implement but modified for battle, the war scythe can be a deadly weapon in the right hands. (Source: PHB)

items.name: Water Opal
items.note: Water Opal is a colourless, clear opal with a play of colour. It is rare and valuable in the Realms, where it is used in scrying devices. (Source: BGII)

items.name: Waterskin of Inexplicable Amelioration
items.note: A sheep's bladder waterskin. It is lined with a suedelike fabric which has been beautifully stiched with the image of a stag's head.

items.name: Yggdrasil Leaf
items.note: A leaf from the great tree Yggdrasil.

items.name: Zulabar's Fist-Stick
items.note: A trolls hand mounted on a three foot rod. It will grasp objects or make a fist. Commands: Grabit, Leggo, Fist.

items.name: 1d20 Ammunition +2
items.note: You have a bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this piece of magic ammunition. The bonus is determined by the rarity of the ammunition. Once it hit a target, the ammunition is no longer magical. Value listed is for individual units. (Source: DMG)

items.name: 1d4 + 4 Beads of Force
items.note: This small black sphere measures 3/4 of an inch in diameter and weighs an ounce. You can use an action to throw the bead up to 60 feet. The bead explodes on impact and is destroyed. Each creature within a 10-foot radius of where the bead landed must succeed on a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw or take 5d4 force damage. A sphere of transparent force then encloses the area for 1 minute. Any creature that failed the save and is completely within the area is trapped inside this sphere. Creatures that succeeded on the save, or are partially within the area, are pushed away from the center of the sphere until they are no longer inside it. Only breathable air can pass through the sphere's wall. No attack or other effect can. An enclosed creature can use its action to push against the sphere's wall, moving the sphere up to half the creature's walking speed. The sphere can be picked up, and it's magic causes it to weigh only 1 pound, regardless of the weight of creatures inside. (Source: DMG)

items.name: A Study of Orcs, by Darius Woodherd
items.note: A large tome bound in thick bison-hide that is a common-orcish language primer, as well as a primer on orcish culture. The author of the primer, Darius Woodherd, seems to have added a lot of information on orcish heraldry and politics, as well. Darius spent almost twenty years amongst the orcs of the north, and eventually married an orc before being killed a score of years ago in a rival tribe’s ambush. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Adamant Leaf
items.note: A large, nearly unbreakable leaf from Tree of Adamant. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Adjatha the Drinker
items.note: This blade belonged to Dabbar, a long dead servant of Bhaal that exercised control over his minions in the most brutal of ways. In addition to strengthening the mind against the guile of others, the sword absorbs life energy from an opponent with each successful hit, healing the user. Dabbar considered it a failure of his officers if he returned from battle in less than perfect health, and as the rest of the company watched, he would administer beatings until fully healed. (Source: Baldur's Gate II)

items.name: Albruin
items.note: Basalin was a large man, rumored, far from his hearing, to be a werebear. He long wielded the bastard sword Albruin in the protection of his village, slaying giant spiders that plagued the area. The sword protected him from their poison and also allowed him to see invisible Red Wizards who controlled some of the more fantastic variants. The reason for their enmity is unknown. (Source: Baldur's Gate II)

items.name: Alexandrite Emerald
items.note: A transparent dark green Alexandrite emerald. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Algernon's Cloak
items.note: Algernon's cloak is imbued with magical properties such that the wearer will seem to shine with an inner glow. (Source: Baldur's Gate II)

items.name: Amulet of Health
items.note: This amulet is a golden disk on a chain. It usually bears the image of a lion or other powerful animal. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Ancient Temple Replica
items.note: Miniature golden replica of ancient mythical temple – location map inside (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Animated Pet Rock
items.note: Animated pet rock – capable of killing people! (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Animated Toy Soldier
items.note: A tiny toy soldier which marches ceaselessly back and forth – eternally vigilant and pissed off if anyone disturbs. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Aphrodisiac Root
items.note: A dirty canvas bag containing twenty pounds of an aphrodisiac root. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Aquamarine
items.note: Aquamarine is a hard, transparent form of beryl, blue-green in colour. These sought after gems are found primarily among the tribes that roam the tundra. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Arbalest
items.note: A large arbalest/bolt thrower. It is disassembled and crated for shipping. It requires four strong men to lift. When reassembled, it hurls a 5’ bolt for 2d12 damage. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Architect's Underwear of Confidence
items.note: This loincloth causes the wearer to have extremely elevated self confidence. As a result, they get +2 on Bluff Diplomacy and Intimidate checks, and -2 on Listen Search Spot and Appraise checks, they also tend to make reckless decisions more often. (Source: AtomicArchitect)

items.name: Armor +1
items.note: You have a +1 bonus to AC while wearing this armor. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Armor of Barbarian Rage
items.note: This is a set of Studded Leather Armour that has been enchanted with the spirit of a long dead barbarian warrior. Once per day it allows the user to channel it's rageful spirit. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Armor of Resistance
items.note: You have resistance to one type of damage while you wear this armor. The DM chooses the type or determines it randomly from the options below. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Armor of Vulnerability
items.note: While wearing this armor, you have resistance to one of the following damage types: bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing. The DM chooses the type or determines it randomly. Curse. This armor is cursed, a fact that is revealed only when an identify spell is cast on the armor or you attune to it. Attuning to the armor curses you until you are targeted by the remove curse spell or similar magic: removing the armor fails to end the curse. While cursed you have vulnerability to two of the three damage type associated with the armor (not the one to which it grants resistance). (Source: DMG)

items.name: Arrow Magnet Amulet
items.note: Whenever an arrow is fired in the vicinity of this amulet’s wearer, it will strike the amulet wearer

items.name: Arrow-catching Shield
items.note: Source: DMG

items.name: Arval's Soundpiece
items.note: Created by the very old and very crotchety wizard Arval long ago, this hollow silver box the size of a deck of playing cards has a rotating dial on top, as well as a button. When the button is pressed, a single target of the wielder's choice is linked with the device magically. By then rotating the dial, the user can alter his target's voice by up to 100%. That is to say the target can be either muted or doubled in volume, and anywhere in between. A will save DC 30 is required to resist the effect. (Source: /u/LittleDizzleDaGusha)

items.name: Backpack of Effortlessness
items.note: Halves the weight of anything placed inside of it. Nothing larger than the backpack can fit inside the backpack. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Bad Coin
items.note: A coin that always comes up tails. Placing it anywhere tails side down causes it to flip itself over after 1 round. It takes 50 pounds of pressure to prevent this flip. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Bag of Angel Feathers
items.note: 1d6 angel feathers aura of good; razor sharp; stronger than steel (can be used as weapon like dagger) (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Bag of Ash
items.note: The Bag of Ash acts like a jar of infinite water, except it just makes a grey, sooty ash. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Bag of Beans
items.note: Inside this heavy cloth bag are 3d4 dry beans. The bag weights 1/2 pound plus 1/4 pound for each bean it contains. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Bag of Hurling
items.note: Source: Uberbeard

items.name: Bag of Tricks, Rust
items.note: This ordinary rust coloured cloth bag appears to be empty. Reaching inside the bag, however, reveals the presence of a small, fuzzy object. You can use an action to pull the fuzzy object from the bag and throw it up to 20 feet. When the object lands, it transforms into a creature you determine by rolling a d8 and consulting the table that corresponds to the bag’s color. The creature vanishes at the next dawn or when it is reduced to 0 hit points. The creature is friendly to you and your companions, and it acts on your turn. You can use a bonus action to command how the creature moves and what action it takes on its next turn, or to give it general orders, such as to attack your enemies. In the absence of such orders, the creature acts in a fashion appropriate to its nature. Once three fuzzy objects have been pulled from the bag, the bag cannot be used again until the next dawn. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Bag of Tricks, Tan
items.note: This ordinary tan cloth bag appears to be empty. Reaching inside the bag, however, reveals the presence of a small, fuzzy object. You can use an action to pull the fuzzy object from the bag and throw it up to 20 feet. When the object lands, it transforms into a creature you determine by rolling a d8 and consulting the table that corresponds to the bag’s color. The creature vanishes at the next dawn or when it is reduced to 0 hit points. The creature is friendly to you and your companions, and it acts on your turn. You can use a bonus action to command how the creature moves and what action it takes on its next turn, or to give it general orders, such as to attack your enemies. In the absence of such orders, the creature acts in a fashion appropriate to its nature. Once three fuzzy objects have been pulled from the bag, the bag cannot be used again until the next dawn. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Ballistic Fist Hammer
items.note: Ballistic Fist Hammer (Hammer with each side acting as ballistic fists from Fallout series) (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Banshee Spirit Dagger
items.note:
Despite appearing to be a normal dagger, even those not able to recognise the presence of magic can tell that there is something imbued in the unassuming metal hilt and blade.

When appraised it transpires that this blade was buried with a banshee and it's spirit has somehow been absorbed. Now whomever uses the dagger carries the risk that its spirit will be unleashed. (Source: /u/Silanoch)

items.name: Belt of Baby Fire Giant Strength
items.note: To all intents and purposes this resembles a Belt of Fire Giant Stength, and it certainly does have a giant's strength enchantment on it; unfortunately, the it was accidentally made with the strength of a baby fire giant. Fire giant babies have roughly the same strength as an average human man. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Belt of Dwarvenkind
items.note:
While wearing this belt, you gain the following benefits: Your Constitution score increases by 2, to a maximum of 20. You have advantage on Charisma (Persuasion) checks made to interact with dwarves.
In addition, while attuned to the belt, you have a 50 percent chance each day at dawn of growing a full beard if you're capable of growing one, or a visibly thicker beard if you already have one.
If you aren't a dwarf, you gain the following additional benefits while wearing the belt: You have advantage on saving throws against poison, and you have resistance against poison damage. You have darkvision out to a range of 60 feet. You can speak, read, and write Dwarvish. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Belt of Hill Giant Strength
items.note:
While wearing this belt, your Strength score changes to 21. If your Strength is already
equal to or greater than 21, the item has no effect on you. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Berserker Axe
items.note: You gain +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. In addition, while you are attuned to this weapon your hit point maximum increased by 1 for each level you have attained. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Bioluminescent Fluid Lantern
items.note: Shake this lantern well and you get 2d4 hours of light. It takes a standard action to shake the lantern and requires 8 hours to 'recharge'. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Black Opal
items.note: Black Opal has a greenish hue with black mottling and gold flecks. Usually found in ancient hot springs, the gem is tumbled smooth and cut cabochon. The phrase in the north "Black as an opal" is used as a subtle form of praise for kind-hearted rogues and the like. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Black Pearl
items.note: A dark blue-black Pearl. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Blacksalt’s Luckmaker
items.note: A worn coin with notched edges. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Bleeding Rock
items.note: A hard, roughly cut piece of basalt rock which bleeds when pressure is applies to it. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Blindfold of Reading
items.note: Allows comprehension of all written languages. Unfortunately, said comprehension is only granted when the blindfold is worn over the eyes - meaning you cannot see to read anything. Perhaps your group can find a work around?

items.name: Blindfold of Trap Awareness
items.note: Semi-reliable Blindfold of Trap Awareness, sends shivers down the wearer’s spine when traps are near – or at random times.

items.name: Bloka Tao's Spoon of Cooking
items.note: A plain wooden spoon that appears to have been used in many, many meals. The spoon comes in a stained walnut box with two brass hinges and a brass hook on the front. No other markings are present. [Any markings made on the box or spoon will disappear in 24 hours. The box itself is magical in nature and will alter any spoon made to the same size of the Spoon of Cooking to become a duplicate of the Spoon of Cooking.] (Source: /u/Bloka2au)

items.name: Blood Scabbard
items.note: A scabbard stained the dark color of rust.

items.name: Blue Sapphire Ring
items.note: A silver ring with a transparent blue-white sapphire. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Blue Spinel Ring
items.note: A silver ring with a Blue Spinel gemstone embedded on it. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Bolt of Chaos
items.note: Crossbow bolt of chaos – 1 random effect on hit (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Bone Crushing Mace
items.note: Bone crushing mace – critical renders limb useless; two handed by any smaller than large

items.name: Bone Stylus
items.note: Write the name of your antagonist in your flesh and he will leave you be. Causes one hit point of damage for every letter inscribed in the bearer's flesh. Lasts until the damage is healed.

items.name: Booming Scepter
items.note: An elegant, decorative hand held scepter made of bronze and small jewels. You can hear a mild humming resonance when held to your ear. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Boots of Feral Leaping
items.note: Plain leather boots, tattered and worn, but glistening with a soft white light similar to the sunlight reflecting off freshly fallen snow. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine via Matt Mercer)

items.name: Boots of Levitation
items.note: While you wear these boots, you can use an action to cast the levitate spell on yourself at will. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Boots of Speed
items.note: While you wear these boots, you can use a bonus action and click the boots' heels together. If you do, the boots double your walking speed, and any creature that makes an opportunity attack against you has disadvantage on the attack roll. If you click your heels together again, you end the effect. When the boots' property has been used for a total of 10 minutes, the magic ceases to function until you finish a long rest. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Bottle
items.note: Once per day, restores 4d2 HP.This goes up to 4d4 at 5, 6d4 at 10,  8d4 at 15 and 10d4 at 20. Cannot use during Combat

items.name: Bottle of Giant's Urine
items.note: This bottle stinks before it's even been opened. The stench of it's contents will protect whomever can stomach to wear it from almost any wandering predators. It's very difficult to get said stench out of adventuring gear, however. Potency may vary on weather, but it should ward of predators for at least a day or two. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Bottle Stopper
items.note: A reusable bottle cork embossed with gold leaf and set with amethysts. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Bowl of Commanding Water Elementals
items.note:
While this bowl is filled with water, you can use an action to speak the bowl's command word and summon a water elemental, as if you had cast the conjure elemental spell. The bowl can't be used this way again
until the next dawn. The bowl is about 1 foot in diameter and half as deep. It weighs 3 pounds and holds about 3 gallons. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Bracers of Defense
items.note: While wearing these bracers, you gain a +2 bonus to AC if you are wearing no armor and using no shield. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Brazier of Commanding Fire Elementals
items.note: While a fire burns in this brass brazier, you can use an action to speak the brazier's command word and summon a fire elemental, as if you had cast the conjure elemental spell. The brazier can't be used this way again until the next dawn. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Buckler of Friendship
items.note: This buckler has been enchanted with the power to protect you and your allies.

items.name: Bullet Bag
items.note: A small bag containing 58 clay sling bullets. Each bullet has a 12 gp opal hidden inside.

items.name: Canary Diamond
items.note: A diamond of incredible beauty which slowly shifts its color from the purest white to canary to blue to black and back again.

items.name: Carlin's Courageous Chickens
items.note: These three chickens have been trained to check for traps, unfortunately they are not very good at it. Once released, each chicken will roam a dungeon at 30 feet a round and will walk in the direction of any nearby traps. If there are no nearby traps, they will walk in the direction that the players imagine there to be traps (they are prone to getting caught up in the moment). Each chicken will accidentally set off any trap it is feasible for it to set off and be killed by the trap. The chicken have name tags and are called Dara, Demi and Miki.

items.name: Ceremonial Dagger
items.note: This is a ceremonial dagger made of electrum with a black pearl in it's pommel. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Chimera's Mantel
items.note: This Cloak is woven together from the fur of a lion and goat over the skin of a serpent. It bears the emblem of house Chimera. (Source: Fin Fen)

items.name: Cinder Glove
items.note: A left-handed glove. It remains warm regardless of atmosphere, and is almost painfully hot to be touched with. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Circlet of Inconsolable Weeping
items.note: Cursed. Anything who puts on this circlet is sent into an irreversible state of misery for 1d4 hours.

items.name: Coiling Rope (50 feet)
items.note: This rope coils itself after 10 rounds of being uncoiled, regardless of what is happening to it. It will undo all knots and will wind itself up around one end (always the same end). This process always takes 1 round.

items.name: Collapsible Rod
items.note: 1’, 10’, or 20’ – any size you need!

items.name: Countdown Cutlass
items.note: A large sword with jewels that sequentially light up after activation. After the last jewel is lit, the sword will explode!

items.name: Cowardly Blade
items.note: A normal, if not well-crafted looking shortsword. There is a sigil of a chicken on the bottom of the pommel. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Crab Crown
items.note: Provides +2 AC when worn, but attracts the attention of crabs, who will come from nearby and cling to the wearer

items.name: Crate of Chaos Mushrooms
items.note: This wooden crate is about 60cm by 60cm by 40cm, with 'Chaos Mushrooms' burned into the lid in Sylvan. It fizzes with a faint magical aura, and smells delicious (Source: /u/Ragatan)

items.name: Crescent Armor Spikes
items.note: A set of Armor Spikes made out of thick, sharp silver. They can be attached to full or half-plate, and behave like normal armor spikes. However, they grow faintly in total darkness - - enough for the wearer to see 5 ft, or for another person to see the wearer from up to 50 ft away. (Source: /u/LittleDizzleDaGusha)

items.name: Crystal Prism
items.note: Crystal prism (d20 x 10 gp)

items.name: Curse Doll
items.note: Anything significant that happens to the doll happens to you, beneficial or harmful.

items.name: Cursed Tarot Cards
items.note: Demonic themed tarot/playing cards – extra planar repercussions based on game played; tarot tales come true

items.name: Dagger of Bleeding
items.note: DC save to avoid bleeding damage

items.name: Dehydrated Minotaur Helm
items.note: A preserved Minotaur head, old but still retaining most detail. The skin is old and decrepit, yet both the horns have been dipped in molten gold. (Source: /u/LittleDizzleDaGusha)

items.name: Digiridoom
items.note: A long, Hollow wooden rod with a narrow opening at one end, and a wider one at the other. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Dowsing Compass
items.note: Like a regular compass, except with multiple needles each pointing toward different useful things, such as: north, the nearest source of fresh water, the strongest nearby evil aura, nearest large concentration of gold, nearest civilization (exact definition of civilization may vary by user), and nearest storm. Needles lengthen and shorten as the object gets closer or further away.

items.name: Dowsing Rod
items.note: Object of secret door detection/lay out visualization

items.name: Dragon Comb Set
items.note: A set of golden combs shapes as dragons, each set with red garnets as the eyes. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Dragon Scale Tombe
items.note: Trained rat in wooden cage – sniffs out gold in exchange for treats. One trail ration per sniff.

items.name: Dreaming Nightfeather
items.note: A long, black feather with violet iridescence. The shaft glows softly white in moonlight. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Elixir of Madness
items.note: Upon taking a single sip of this potion, you are afflicted with some form of madness. Roll 1d6. On a 1-3 you are afflicted with a short-term madness (DMG p. 259); on a 4-5 you are afflicted with a long-term madness (DMG p. 260); on a 6 you are afflicted with an indefinite madness.(DMG p. 260). The remainder of the elixir loses its magical properties, becoming merely a foul tasting liquid. (Source: D&D Wiki)

items.name: Embroidered Gloves
items.note: An embroidered glove set designed for human sized hands, each with jewel chips. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Emerald
items.note: An Emerald is a brilliant green beryl, cleaved along straight box like lines, with rectangular cutting in the finished gem. Emeralds are often connected with health, and so are used ornamentally for such a purpose. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Exceptionally Roguish Hat
items.note: A wide, leather hat, garnished with gold-inlaid thread and exotic bird feathers. Very Vogue. (Source: Lizardmaaaaan)

items.name: Extra-Dimensional Flask
items.note: Extra dimensional flask – holds 1 gallon

items.name: Eye Patch
items.note: An eye patch with a mock eye set in blue sapphire and moonstone. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Filligins Uncontrollable Flame
items.note: A small wooden box with a silver embossed flame on the lid. Inside is a pipe-shape horn that spouts a tiny flame that can not be snuffed. The box is not flammable. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Filthy Bucket
items.note: A filthy bucket, bolted to the floor, ¾ full of urine, containing silver coins, rings and necklaces.

items.name: Fire Blanket
items.note: A heavy rug that has been kept rolled up. When unrolled, it reveals that the surface of the rug is permanently on fire. When rolled back up the fire disappears. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Fire Opal
items.note: This Fire Opal is a translucent fiery red colour and is set within a broken brooch. It takes no effort to wedge it out. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Fire Opal Chain
items.note: A fine gold chain set with a fire opal. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Flail of Flagellation
items.note: This imposing, three-headed flail has a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls. Any creature that takes damage from this flail may immediately make a saving throw against any one ongoing effect that is affecting them. (Source: Scribnibbler21)

items.name: Frostbite Lilly
items.note: A pure white lily that becomes rigid when picked. Its petals are icy cold and shatter like glass if mishandled. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Gale Boomerang
items.note: This oddly-shaped weapon looks like it might be made of roc’s feathers, bound together and strengthened by magic. There is a small emerald set in the weapon’s vertex amidst gilded furnishings that mark it as a weapon of some distinction. (Source: D&D Wiki)

items.name: Gauntlets
items.note: The curse of the gauntlets was that no magic would ever help you, beneficial or baneful and anything you were in contact with would lose its magical properties while you're touching it. found out the contact one when visiting flying mage's tower. I was no longer welcome there, but with certain classes the gloves are pretty sweet even considering the downside. Oh also the curse had a very specific trigger, the gauntlets could only be removed if you cast remove curse on them and then had a chaotic neutral halfling pull them off of you with the intention of stealing them. even if you hacked your arms off and then had regeneration cast on you your hands would grow back with the gauntlets on them.

items.name: Giant Temple Candles
items.note: Six giant temple candles. Each is 6’ tall, 1’ thick, and weighs 100 lbs. They are made of fine white wax, rolled in gold dust, and set with emeralds. (320 gps each)

items.name: Girdle of Femininity/Masculinity
items.note: The cursed Girdle of Femininity and Masculinity, a rare yet oft-discussed magical item, is wrought with a most powerful magic. Anyone unfortunate enough to secure it around their waist instantly finds their gender to be transformed. It is said that not fifty years past, a nameless court jester was beheaded for presenting the girdle as a gift to Duke Lobelahn's lover. (Source: Baldur's Gate)

items.name: Goeric's Bountiful Creel
items.note: A woven basket with a loose and wide brim, tapering at the bottom, and made from finely plaited reeds. A triangular canvas flap is sewn to the top of the basket, which is well-worn from use but still a crisp white. A simple embroidery has been made in the center of this flap; a fish attached to a string and pole. A dark leather strap is attached at the far ends of the basket, suitable for draping over the shoulder so that one can carry the creel at the hip. (Source: Dozus)

items.name: Gold Circlet
items.note: A gold circlet set with four aquamarines. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Gold Idol
items.note: A small gold idol. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Gold Plated Idols
items.note: Gold plated idols – possession = death penalty (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Grog of Substantial Whimsy
items.note: http://www.dandwiki.com/wiki/Grog_of_Susbtantial_Whimsy_%283.5e_Equipment%29

items.name: Hammer of Thunderous Power
items.note: Thunder Hammer – deafening or stunned, DC save, 40’ radius

items.name: Harp
items.note: A carved harp of exotic wood with an ivory inlay and zircon gems. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Headband of Enslavement
items.note: Description needed.

items.name: Headdress of the Dawn Dancer
items.note: At the end of a long rest, a Bard attuned to this item may perform a dancing ritual while wearing it. That bard makes a Performance check. Each creature that watches the whole ritual performance may replace their result for one attack roll, ability check, or saving throw with the result of the bard's performance check. The ritual performance takes 5 minutes to complete. (Source: Scribnibbler21)

items.name: Helm of Telepathy
items.note: A bronze sallet-style helmet shaped so that the crown resembles a brain. A purple eyepiece is embedded into the visor, enabling the wearer to gaze through it. (Source: SRD)

items.name: Hollow Glass Sphere
