items.note: A hollow glass sphere of surprising proportions – nearly three meters across. If it is broken those nearby will catch the barest scent of alien perfumes, hinting at strange lands belonging to the ancient time when the sphere was first forged and air trapped within it.

items.name: Hyperstone
items.note: Once per day this item grants one extra attack for it's user.

items.name: Insanity Blade
items.note: Source: William Orfanakos

items.name: Javelin of Explosions
items.note: 5x range, normal range 5x dmg, 2xs range = 4xs dmg, 3xs range = 3xs dmg, etc… - explodes & destroyed after use except at 5xs or 4xs range

items.name: Jeweled Anklet
items.note: A jeweled anklet. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Lightning Rod
items.note: What once might have once been a simple spear has been transformed by a series of intricate Lichtenberg figures that run down the wooden shaft. These intricate, branching designs resemble miniature lightning strikes or the spread of a fern and work their way down from the copper spearhead. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Lost Diamond
items.note: A sizeable cut diamond with bluish tint. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Maester Sword
items.note: A shiny silver sword with a purple hilt. There are three orange pommels at the center of the hilt that each resembles a triangle, placed together so they create a larger triangle as a group. The guard is shaped like two outstretched wings and along the blade of this sword are etchings in Elvish that read "The Blade of Evil's Bane". Looking into the history of this sword will tell of an order of Maesters who, upon growing weary of merely recording the passage of history, decided to make it themselves. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Magmatic Elixir
items.note: A viscous potion in a clear glass bottle. It is uncomfortably hot to hold, and glows a faint orange in darkness. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Magnetic Grappling Hook
items.note: Self-guided grappling hook - +10 to ranged attack roll for aiming

items.name: Malluf's Not-Quite-Full, Not-Quite-Empty Quiver
items.note: Near-Empty-Ever-Full One Arrow Quiver – always only 1 arrow, will reappear after being shot.

items.name: Mantle Set
items.note: An embroidered silk and velvet mantle set with numerous moonstones. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Masterpiece Painting
items.note: An old painting, a lot treasure by a painter of some renoun. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Mongrel Collar
items.note: A black leather collar, stiff from wear and riddled with bite marks. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Mr. Hammer's Crazy Staff
items.note: A staff that could change into a hammer and give him extra strength while it was in that mode.

items.name: Music Box
items.note: An ornate golden music box. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Navigational Calculator
items.note: A gear-driven ships navigational calculator enclosed in a jewelwood box.

items.name: Necromancer's Saddle
items.note: Black leather saddle with streaks of red through the leather itself. A small amount of black smoke emanates from the saddle when it is holding still. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: New Year's Sword
items.note: The silver hilt of a sword with a grip of black leather. The blade is missing, or never existed to begin with. One night per year, however, on the eve of the new year, the hilt is possessed of a ghostly blade – allowing it to perform its duties as a sword once more. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Nightmare Pipeweed
items.note: A common looking pipeweed, which smells only a little sweeter than the most common varieties. Close inspection reveals occasional black burrs mixed among the leaves. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Obsidian Charm
items.note: A charm of twisted black obsidian. Touching the charm unleashes strange, ghostly visions from a strange and alien world – utterly different in every particular, but eerily similar to our own world in its broad scope and form.

items.name: Obsidian Statuette
items.note: An obsidian statuette of a demon with a golden inlay and fittings. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Olivewood Staff
items.note: A very heavy, hard staff engrained with beautiful swirling, natural patterns across the wood. It has a large diameter and is slightly tapered to be thicker at one end. It can be used like a normal quarterstaff, except at 1d8 damage. However, in the hands of a Druid or a 5th-level Ranger, the staff's thicker tip can be lit at-will like a Sunrod of any color. The wielder is also capable of easily distinguishing any non-natural (magical) trees, and can speak Entish (so long as they could already speak Elvish). (Source: /u/LittleDizzleDaGusha)

items.name: Opal
items.note: A translucent pale blue Opal with green and golden mottling. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Orb of Artificers Intelligence
items.note: A sphere of pearlescent metal, shaped like a knot of looping segments that twist into each other. A small note was left alongside the orb with two command words, which seemingly activate and deactivate it. (Source: OneManBland)

items.name: Ornate Longsword
items.note: This longsword seems to have been made more for show than for use. It is silver-plated steel and has a jet gemstone set inside the hilt. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Outdated Book of Prophecies
items.note: A small, leather-bound book filled with prophecies. All of them will be found to be true, but the last of them is dated just a few weeks ago. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Owlbear Costume
items.note: An owlbear costume. Gives the wearer a +10 to disguise when attempting to disguise as an Owlbear.

items.name: Pearl Necklace
items.note: A necklace string of small pink pearls. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Peridot Gemstone
items.note: A circular Peridot of rich green colouring with milky inclusions. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Philter of Blave
items.note: A viscous, golden liquid inside of a corked glass vial. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Phoenix Choker
items.note: Ash of phoenix in necklace choker – instant revive to wearer (takes 10 rounds/1 minute)

items.name: Phoenix Restorative Potion
items.note: Potion 3: Phoenix Restorative. The next time the drinker is reduced to 0 hit points, they deal area fire damage, and return to full health, only happens once. The player has no special knowledge this is true, but the flavor of the draught burns their throat but leaves them feeling oddly optimistic. Fire damage dealt should be based on circumstance, but if you can get away with lots, it'd be the most fun. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Platinum Bracelet
items.note: A platinum bracelet set with a sapphire. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Potion of Extra Health
items.note: A foul smelling liquid that shifts between bright red and burgundy as it swirls around it's flask. (Source: Unknown)

items.name: Potion of Purple Ooze
items.note: A potion vial containing a purple concoction, labelled simply "Ooze". The bubbling, slimy fluid is dark purple with emerald accretions on the side. It smells like butyric acid (vomit) and tastes worse. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Pouch of Healing Potions
items.note: A large carry pouch made from the skin of a monkey. Inside are 1d6 Potions of Superior Healing. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Power Treads
items.note: Grants the following, +2 fort saves, +2 reflex saves, +2 will saves (Source: DOTA2)

items.name: Pregnancy Figure
items.note: The tiny figurine of a fertility goddess, carved from a rare, fist-sized pearl. Found deep beneath the surface of the earth, for two weeks after it is brought to the surface any woman touching the figurines will become pregnant. After the two week period has expired, the figure will never work again (or until returned to its original spot for 1d12 months).

items.name: Prism of Paranoia
items.note: This cursed item hovers just behind whoever it is attuned to, generating startling sounds and lights at random intervals. A character attuned to the Prism of Paranoia cannot sleep or benefit from long or short rests, but has +10 to passive Perception. (Source: Scribnibbler21)

items.name: Rafting Cloak
items.note: An cherrywood cloak that repels water perfectly. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Ring Mail of Three Wishes
items.note: This armor is Leather Armor with heavy rings sewn into it. The rings help reinforce the armor against blows from swords and axes. Three rings across the chest are made of brilliant gold that sheds dim light similar to daybreak. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Ring of Contrariness
items.note: Source: 2nd Ed.

items.name: Ring of Ghosts
items.note: Ring of Ghosts: the wearer of the ring can see and communicate with the ghost of any intelligent being that has died in the past 24 hours within a 1 mile radius. After the 24 hours, the ghost disappears. The ghosts are only visible and audible to the wearer, but remain intangible. Also, the ghosts likely aren't very cooperative if the players killed them.

items.name: Rod of Rulership
items.note: You can use an action to present the rod and command obedience from each creature of your choice that you can see within 120 feet of you. Each target must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or be charmed by you for 8 hours. While charmed in this way, the creature regards you as its trusted leader. If harmed by you or your companions, or commanded to do something contrary to its nature, a target ceases to be charmed in this way. The rod can't be used again until the next dawn.

items.name: Rust-Dust Bomb
items.note: Rust-dust Bomb – metallic eating dust

items.name: Scroll of Cursed Gibberish
items.note: Without a successful Arcana check of 25, this scroll appears to be a Scroll of Comprehend Language. When used, however a DC save must be made or the user will be unable to speak anything but gibberish for 1d20 days.

items.name: Scroll of Summon Moose
items.note: Summons a moose.

items.name: Self-Incinerating Pellets
items.note: extra planar/dimensional transport/escape to pocket dimension with scrying/teleportation stone/orb

items.name: Shadoweave Armor
items.note: Shadoweave armor is woven with darkness imbued into its silk. +6 armor +6 max bonus

items.name: Shield of Bashing
items.note: A tough metal shield with a large stud in the center. (Source: Brendan M)

items.name: Silver and Gold Brooch
items.note: A simple but finely made silver and gold brooch. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Silver Chalice
items.note: A silver chalice set with moonstones. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Silver Platter of Feathers
items.note: A light silver platter, 6 inches in diameter, etched with ornate feathers. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Spear of the Hunter
items.note: Source: William Orfanakos

items.name: Spire of the Manticore
items.note:
You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls with this magical weapon, and it deals an additional 1d8 poison damage. The damage increases by 1d8 if the target is a Monstrosity or Beast.

This weapon has 7 spikes hidden within the Barb at the end of the pike. As a ranged attack, you can launch a single spike at a target, each having a range of 100/200 ft. Each of these spikes deal 1d8 piercing damage. Every Morning at dawn, this weapon regrows all of it's spikes. (Source: Fin Fen)

items.name: Star of Morning's Glory
items.note: This Morningstar is made of solid wrought steel, with a heavy spiked head. It behaves like a normal Morningstar, except within 60ft of Evil and Undead creatures and within an hour of Dawn. In any of these cases, the head glows like the tip of a Sunrod, and it becomes a Morningstar +2 which stuns for 1d4+1 rnds on a Critical. Fortitude saves vs stun, with a DC equal to the player's attack roll. Cannot be used by Evil creatures. (Source: /u/LittleDizzleDaGusha)

items.name: Star Ruby
items.note: A translucent ruby with a white star-shaped center. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Star Sapphire
items.note: Star Sapphire is a precious variation of the sapphire, however is more translucent, with a white star highlighted in its center. A star sapphire has been known to ornament devices which protect against magic. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Sting
items.note: Monster presence detecting weapon (Hobbit – Sting, Orcs/Goblins)

items.name: Stones of Ruin
items.note: A simple leather pouch filled with 4 small runestones. (Source: /u/Euphorbus11)

items.name: Sun Gem
items.note: This round and polished stone glows with a fierce light and seems to dispel the shadows around you. (Source: BGII)

items.name: Sword of Bleeding
items.note: A sharp one-sided blade that is slightly curved. (Source: Brendan M)

items.name: Telescoping Glaive
items.note: This is a magical Glaive, with an ornately decorated shaft made of solid (but light) steel. The blade is made of silver, and floats near the end unattached to the shaft. Three keywords act on this weapon: anda, sinta, and pahta (long, short, and closed, in Elven). Anda is the longest extent, and the weapon acts as a two-handed glaive. Sinta makes the weapon shorten and behave as a one-handed axe, and pahta shortens the weapon to throwing-axe size. The blade also changes shape and position to fit these weapon styles. (Source: /u/LittleDizzleDaGusha)

items.name: The Clegane Bowl
items.note: This large basin has bloodstains permanently ingrained in it. Any liquid placed in the Clegane Bowl immediately turns into Potion of Greater Hype. When consumed, a dose of this potion doubles the Strength score of the user for 1 hour, up to a maximum of 30 Strength. (Source: Scribnibbler21)

items.name: The Diamond of Sorrow
items.note: This invaluable gem was once a decorative allure grown and used by a mimic. A rumor persists that the diamond got it's name due to an adventurer who befriended, and then eventually betrayed, the mimic that created it. (Source: Fable II)

items.name: The Eye of Patch Adams
items.note: A small grey pewter orb which can replace a characters missing/removed eye. It allows the user to see the internal workings of all creatures, even if they are wearing armour, you can identify organs, bones and anomalies. (Source: Boba Irid)

items.name: The Harbinger
items.note: An elegant longsword that seems to shift it's appearance to better fit the moral character of it's wielder. A history check will reveal that this longsword was forged by the twins Elda and Essa. After its creation, they used it to cut off each others' hands, ensuring that the world would never see another weapon like it. (Source: Fable)

items.name: The Iron Thorn
items.note: This wrought-iron band is pitted and stained as if by acid. Between the two thorns formed by the band, a crude lump of silver on its head suggests the image of a skull against a black field, perhaps the symbol of Myrkul, Lord of Bones. (Source: BG)

items.name: Tome of Alchemy
items.note: A large tome, bound in leather and clasped with gold. A closer inspection of this volume will reveal that each page is perfectly preserved dragon scale which has been inscribed with the black blood of a fiend. The book describes the four spirits (quicksilver, orpiment, sal ammoniac, sulfur) and seven bodies (gold, silver, iron, quicksilver, lead, tin, copper) of traditional alchemy. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Topaz
items.note: A yellow facet cut Topaz gemstone. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Tracking Dagger
items.note: Source: William Orfanakos

items.name: Uncut Diamond
items.note: An uncut chunk of raw diamond. It is easily the size of a dwarf’s fist and is likely worth a substantial sum to the right jewel maker.

items.name: Undead Knife
items.note: Knife of a god of undeath. It turned whoever owned it immediately undead.

items.name: Urn of Shadows
items.note: Urn of shadows-starts with no charges, user can grant allies 10HP, or deal 10 damage to enemies. gains additional uses for every 2 creatures that die within 40ft radius of holder (Source: DOTA)

items.name: Wand of Arcane Errata
items.note: A wand with runic inscriptions that have been scratched out and rewritten several times (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Wand of Prestidigitation
items.note: A black and white painted Oakwood wand, with card suits painted down the length of the wand beyond the handle. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: War Mask
items.note: This mask has been painted gold and appears to be a ceremonial mask worn to battle. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Wellwizard's Teacups
items.note: A pair of elegantly decorated teacups that always feel warm to the touch. If filled with any liquid, it will somehow seep through invisible cracks in the cup. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Whispering Bauble
items.note: A glass orb with a small slit opening, braced by fine grey metal. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Worldly Tortoise
items.note: A small tortoise (STR 2, DEX 1, WIL 6, 1hp, Armour 1) that knows the way to anywhere you ask, but gets there very slowly.

items.name: Yellow Sapphire
items.note: A transparent fiery yellow or yellow· green Sapphire. (Source: DMG)

items.name: 1d20 Ammunition +3
items.note: You have a bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this piece of magic ammunition. The bonus is determined by the rarity of the ammunition. Once it hit a target, the ammunition is no longer magical. Value listed is for individual units. (Source: DMG)

items.name: 2d4 + 2 Arrows of Slaying
items.note: An arrow of slaying is a magic weapon meant to slay a particular kind of creature. Some are more focused than others; for example, there are both arrows of dragon slaying and arrows of blue dragon slaying. If a creature belonging to the type, race, or group associated with an arrow of slaying takes damage from the arrow, the creature must make a DC 17 Constitution saving throw, taking an extra 6d10 piercing damage on a failed save, or half as much extra damage on a successful one. Once an arrow of slaying deals its extra damage to a creature, it becomes a nonmagical arrow. Other types of magic ammunition of this kind exist, such as bolts of slaying meant for a crossbow, though arrows are most common. Value listed is for each arrow. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Amulet of the Planes
items.note:
While wearing this amulet, you can use an action to name a location that you are familiar with on another plane of existence. Then make a DC 15 Intelligence check. On a successful check, you cast the plane shift spell. On a failure , you and each creature and object within 15 feet of you travel to a random destination.
Roll a dlOO. On a 1- 60, you travel to a random location on the plane you named. On a 61- 100, you travel to a randomly determined plane of existence. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Amulets of Inedibility
items.note: These two amulets are enchanted as a pair and both must be worn at the same time for the enchantment to work. Anything wearing it cannot be considered as food. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Anderson's Medallion
items.note: Once per day, you can remove AC of target granted by armor, last until end of your next turn. User takes -2 to AC for same duration. (I think lasting until the end of combat might be a bit op, also  I have named it after Hans Christian Anderson because it makes me thing of the Emperor's New Clothes) (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Animated Rope
items.note: Smart 50’ rope – obeys simple commands, animated like snake (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Animated Shield
items.note: Source: DMG

items.name: Archon's Dream
items.note: It is said that this one was presented to an ancient ruler named Archon in a dream, as a gift from the gods. After the destruction of his kingdom, three of these "divinity" gems were scattered throughout the world, and though they have often been dismissed as nothing more than legends, thousands have devoted their lives to finding them. (Source: Fable II)

items.name: Armor +2
items.note: You have a +2 bonus to AC while wearing this armor. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Bag of Devouring
items.note: This bag superficially resembles a bag of holding but is a feeding orifice for a gigantic extradimensional creature. Turning the bag inside out closes the orifice. The extradimensional creature attached to the bag can sense whatever is placed inside the bag. Animal or vegetable matter placed wholly in the bag is devoured and lost forever. When part of a living creature is placed in the bag, as happens when someone reaches inside it, there is a 50 percent chance that the creature is pulled inside the bag. A creature inside the bag can use its action to try to escape with a successful DC 15 Strength check. Another creature can use its action to reach into the bag to pull a creature out, doing so with a successful DC 20 Strength check (provided it isn't pulled inside the bag first). Any creature that starts its turn inside the bag is devoured, its body destroyed. Inanimate objects can be stored in the bag, which can hold a cubic foot of such material. However, once each day, the bag swallows any objects inside it and spits them out into another plane of existence. The DM determines the time and plane. If the bag is pierced or torn, it is destroyed, and anything contained within it is transported to a random location on the Astral Plane. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Bardweave Parisol
items.note: A beautiful, vibrantly colorful cloth parasol with a light wooden handle. The object feels delicate and weightless. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Belt of Fire Giant Strength
items.note:
While wearing this belt, your Strength score changes to 25. If your Strength is already
equal to or greater than 25, the item has no effect on you. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Belt of Frost Giant Strength
items.note:
While wearing this belt, your Strength score changes to 23. If your Strength is already
equal to or greater than 23, the item has no effect on you. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Belt of Stone Giant Strength
items.note:
While wearing this belt, your Strength score changes to 23. If your Strength is already
equal to or greater than 23, the item has no effect on you. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Black Obelisk
items.note: A plain, unadorned black obelisk. If brought above ground and into the open, the obelisk will draw down a near-constant barrage of lightning to itself – even if the sky is clear. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Black Sapphire
items.note: A translucent lustrous black Sapphire with glowing highlights. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Blink Dagger
items.note: Twice per day, whomever users this dagger can instantly teleport up to 30 ft in any direction. Cannot be used if you were attacked since your last turn.

items.name: Blood Scroll
items.note: A scroll that when signed with blood would enter the participant into an assassin ranking system. Upon the scroll was also an arrow that always pointed to the assassin that was a rank above you. The goal is to reach rank 1 for a single wish however the only way to gain a rank is to kill the assassin above you. The Rank is infamous and well paid for their trade so strong assassins are always waiting for a rank opening. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Bloodied Harp
items.note: An old looking brass harp that has only 3 strings left. The strings are flecked with rust, or so it seems. Holding it gives you a feeling of cold anxiety. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Boots of Dry Land
items.note: The wearer of the boots gains water-walking, with a twist. On any body of salt water, the boots generate a 30 ft. radius of dry, walkable sand beneath the wearer. Which means several party members could benefit from the effect. They functioned for an hour per day, but it didn't have to be continuous.

items.name: Brazier of Infinite Imps
items.note: Brazier of infinite imps. 1d4 imps spawn every 24 hours. If they are not killed by the time the brazier spawns a new set, the previous imps die and vanish.

items.name: Buzzing Buckler
items.note: A wooden buckler with a burned-in sigil of a bee on the shield face. It vibrates ferociously when struck. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Candle of Invocation
items.note: This slender taper is dedicated to a deity and shares that deity's alignment. The candle's alignment can be detected with the detect evil and good spell. The DM chooses the god and associated alignment or determines the alignment randomly. The candle's magic is activated when the candle is lit, which requires an action. After burning for 4 hours, the candle is destroyed. You can snuff it out early for use at a later time. Deduct the time it burned in increments of 1 minute from the candle's total burn time. While lit, the candle sheds dim light in a 30-foot radius. Any creature within that light whose alignment matches that of the candle makes attack rolls, saving throws, and ability checks with advantage. In addition, a cleric or druid in the light whose alignment matches the candle's can cast 1st-level spells he or she has prepared without expending spell slots, though the spell's effect is as if cast with a 1st-level slot. Alternatively, when you light the candle for the first time, you can cast the gate spell with it. Doing so destroys the candle. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Companion Rings
items.note: Two rings, user may opt to take damage for the other person wearing the ring

items.name: Crown of Fingers
items.note: A crown of severed, mummified fingers. Each finger bears three jeweled rings.

items.name: Crystal Pyramid
items.note: A finely-crafted, clear crystal pyramid with no noticeable impurities.

items.name: Cursed Eye
items.note: Allows a blind user to see but at a cost. (will at random cause you to attack others) (Source: asdf3011)

items.name: Dark Gemini Ring
items.note: A set of dark blue rings, which rotate freely against each other, but cannot be separated. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Devil's Disk
items.note: Concave disk of black glass, 2 feet wide, made from obsidian. Etched with Infernal symbols. (Source: Lizardmaaaaan)

items.name: Diamond
items.note: Diamond is a hard clear gem which can be clear blue-white, rich blue, yelow or pink. The hardest of all the gemstones, and among the most valuable. Diamonds are usually found in northern mountains by underground races, and then traded to the surface world. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Donkey Legs
items.note: These wooden donkey legs can be attached to anything  (but are not strong enough to carry literally anything).  Once attached, the thing will follow you around like a loyal donkey.  For example, you can attach them to a heavy statue and then have it walk itself out of the dungeon.  Donkey legs are as strong as two donkeys! (Source: /u/SPYROHAWK)

items.name: Earthbreaker Totem
items.note: A thick shaft of wood with totemic carvings. It has a slight gradual spike shape, being thinner and sharper at one end, and thick and flat at the other. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Eldhand's Mind Slug
items.note: In the laboratory, vault, or home of the famed (and thought long dead) Eldhand the Scholar sits a small glass box. Across the lid is a grid of very tiny holes for air. The box contains a pink, slimy slug admidst some wet sawdust. A tag on the box reads “Eldhand.” The bug can be killed easily, like any slug. However, placing the slug near one's nose or mouth allows it to crawl up into the player's brain. The player will go into a coma for 3d4 hours and, upon awakening, will find that the voice of Eldhand the Scholar can be heard inside their head. He is quite sarcastic, pessimistic, and usually upset that someone so “pea-brained” ended up finding him. However, he can be convinced to give help in scholarly matters—granting the host +6 on all Knowledge Arcana, Knowledge History, and Spellcraft checks. He is reluctant to give out help unless you have proven yourself a more than worthy host. But he is generally willing to help in exchange for your player eating some odd or expensive food item (plums, goat cheese, and Ruby Port liquor tend to be his favorites). (Source: /u/LittleDizzleDaGusha)

items.name: Encyclopedia Approximatus
items.note: A large, unmarked blue tome. Every page inside is blank. (Source: /u/BardicFire)

items.name: Essence Extractor
items.note: Extract the consciousness of a pacified or willing being to be stored within. The consciousness can be injected into the still warm body of a dead being, giving them a new body to inhabit.

items.name: Fulcrum Ring
items.note: A plain, heavy ring of dark metal. It seems occasionally heavy. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Gladiator's Prizeblade
items.note: A high quality, perfectly balanced gladius that is excessively comfortable to wield. The blade only seems to sharpen with use. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Gold Crown
items.note: A jeweled gold crown of superb quality. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Gold Jewelry Box
items.note: A solid gold jewelry box with a platinum filigree. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Gold Statuette
items.note: A small gold statuette of a man, set with rubies. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Harkol's Agonizing Instruments
items.note: A cloth tool-roll that contains six needles of varying thickness. A melody is embroidered on the inside of the roll. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Heavenly Armor
items.note:
A set of enhanced, heavenly armor. Powerful enough to protect and shield a hero on his journey through the the world, or even beyond the Gates of Hell itself. These armors are made for the most faithful and divine mortals and angels alike.
While wearing this armor, you gain a +2 bonus to AC, you have advantage on all saving throws against effects caused by fiends, and it creates an aura in a 5-foot radius around you. Fiends in the aura have disadvantage on saving throws.

items.name: Helm of Brilliance
items.note: A dazzling barbute-style helmet made of polished silver and adorned on every surface with diamonds, rubies, opals and fire opals. This helmet is not just an ostentacious expression of wealth; even those unattuned with magic can feel the power imbued in it. (Source: SRD)

items.name: Helm of Teleportation
items.note: A kabuto styled helmet from the eastern realms made of curved sheets of leather hanging from a hard metal cap. A decorative bronze maedate is fixed to the front of the crown, perhaps a personal crest of symbol of it's creator's clan. (Source: SRD)

items.name: Helpless Bandage
items.note: A person bandaged with this item will be unable to do anything for themselves and will rely on others for everything. They will not remove the bandage until the wound is healed.

items.name: Ill-Fated Necklace
items.note: The wearer of this necklace has a general feeling that he is safe. The opposite is, in fact, the case. Any time something bad happens to the group, the wearer of this necklace bears the brunt of the misfortune

items.name: Ivory Drinking Horn
items.note: A bejeweled ivory drinking horn with gold filigree. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Jacinth
items.note: A transparent fiery orange gem variety of Zircon. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Jade Game Board
items.note: An ornate game board made of jade, with 40 solid gold playing pieces. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Key Dagger
items.note: Appears to be a normal silvered dagger with some cuts running up it’s spine.

items.name: King's Tears
items.note: King's Tears are very rare and are sometimes called frozen tears. Clear, teardrop-shaped, smooth surfaced, and totally unbreakable so far by any means. These stones are said to be the crystallized tears of long-dead necromancer kings. In each gem can be seen that which the weeping king loved long ago. Their true nature is unknown but suffice it to say that sages prize these gems above all others (Source: BGII)

items.name: Links of Truth
items.note: Plain, but exceptionally clean chainmail that feels light, and always cold. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Mother's Love
items.note: A gold-rimmed cobalt locket inscribed with draconic runes. Inside is a small replaceable portrait. (Source: /u/BrainBlowX)

items.name: Mysterious Dwarven Club Hammer
items.note: A mysterious ornamental club hammer of dwarf origin, only someone with extensive knowledge of weapons or magic (Eg. Blacksmith, Enchanter, etc) can discover its true meaning. (Source: Kokkusu)

items.name: Necklace of the Fang Pact
items.note: This fiber necklace contains beads that separate several teeth from a variety of creatures. All together, it can support up to 7 teeth. (Source: Fin Fen)

items.name: Nevermore Seed
items.note: A small pouch of tiny dry seeds that leave black soot on your fingers and smell of pungent incense. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Permanently Damp Rag
items.note: A damp rag which carries a faint musty smell.

items.name: Phamea's Pocket Steed
items.note: This is a small brass whistle, shaped like the head of a horse and stained with age. When the whistle is blown an extremely high-pitched note is emitted, summoning a magical steed that serves just like a Heavy Warhorse. It will stay until the whistle is blown again—at which time it unsummons. However, if the horse is slain, its body disappears, and the whistle will never emit a sound again. (Source: /u/LittleDizzleDaGusha)

items.name: Pink Nayla Petal
items.note: A thick, velvety flower petal of pastel pink and flecks of red. It leaves a fragrant, pleasant dust on your fingertips. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Platinum Ring
items.note: A jeweled platinum ring. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Polymorphed Adventurers Potions
items.note: The potions are a polymorphed group of famous heroes transformed into oozes. Anyone with the ability to communicate telepathically are able to speak with them (and probably help them get back to normal). (Source: DangerousPuhson)

items.name: Potion of Lycanthropy Immunization
items.note: A thick green potion in a small clear bottle. Closer examination reveals silver particles suspended in the liquid. (Source: D&D Wiki)

items.name: Potion of Sudden Translocation
items.note: Inside this strangely shaped vial swirls a blackish purple liquid. When used it will randomly and instantly teleport the user to a free spot up to 50 feet away. This includes through walls, into pits and between floors. However the potion will not work if you are otherwise prevented from teleporting either by Dimensional shackles or other by means. (Source: D&D Wiki)

items.name: Pouch of Healing Potions
items.note: A large carry pouch made from the skin of a monkey. Inside are 1d6 Potions of Superior Healing. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Powder of Piercing
items.note: A gray powder that when thrown vastly increases speed and creates many small holes in the target (Source: asdf3011)

items.name: Psyche of Strife
items.note: A tall mirror in ornate silvery frame with perfectly reflecting surface. The ornaments depicting faceless humanoids locked in battle. (Source: Rutin75)

items.name: Pyramid Model
items.note: A miniature model of a pyramid, which glows a faint blue. If a deceased creature of Diminutive or Fine size is placed beneath the pyramid, they will be reincarnated in 1d20 hours. The pyramid can manifest this ability once per lunar month.

items.name: Quelling Axe
items.note: This axe appears to be like any other felling axe, but when used it will unerringly cut down any tree with one swing. Its formidable weight and uneven balancing make it a slow, inefficient weapon. (Source: DOTA2)

items.name: Reanimation Bomb
items.note: Re-animation bomb

items.name: Remnant
items.note: "The handle was made of black leathers which connected two chains down to the head of a cylinder-shaped flail head. The head was like a cage that had interlocking strips of steel with spikes pointing outwards at every link. Inside, it contained a brilliant blue light that hummed which grew with intensity when spun." (Source: Fin Fen)

items.name: Ring of Returning
items.note: Sometimes called a "Back to the Beginning" ring. This is a band of cold silvery metal, the ring looks deceptively simple at first glance. Only when held close do faint runes reveal themselves—etched so finely they seem to shimmer in and out of sight, like ripples on still water. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Ring of Sustenance
items.note: Ring of Sustenance – require no food/water/rations

items.name: Rogue Stone
items.note: A small, shifting, rainbow coloured, irridecent gemstone. The fluid shades of colour appear almost liquid under normal sunlight, it is truly a beautiful sight to behold. Rogue stones are extremely rare and are used for the gemjump spell, hence one of the most sought after gems in the Realms. (Source: BGII)

items.name: Ruby
items.note: Ruby is a pink to blood-red colored gemstone, a variety of the mineral corundum. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Sarcophagus
items.note: A painted gold child's sarcophagus. It is empty, but itself valuable. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Set of Golden Cups
items.note: A set of golden cups, each set with emeralds. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Staff of the Verdant Grove
items.note: A knotted and well-worn walking stick with living vines that grow from it. There are druidic symbols for everlasting life etched near the handle. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Stern Sword of Grammar Correction
items.note: A dull, rather drab looking rapier inhabited with the intelligence of a hyper-particular grammarian. (Source: Jeff C)

items.name: Stick of Rage
items.note: A carved, thumb-width stick of great sturdiness. Holding it fills you with a furious urge to break it. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Stone of Myr'Bregothil
items.note: It is said this gem was once imbued with the power to prevent those who died from passing over to the next realm. Whether it is true or not, it remains extremely valuable. (Source: Fable II)

items.name: Sunfire Chakrams
items.note: A set of two Chakrams, one copper and the other bronze, both wrapped with old leather handles. Each deals 1d6 damage and an additional +2 Fire damage, when either thrown or swung in melee. If a Chakram is thrown and hits, it returns to the thrower's hand by magical means. Treated as an exotic weapon. 1.5lbs each (Source: /u/LittleDizzleDaGusha)

items.name: Sunfire Shurikens
items.note: This is a bag of shurikens, made of flat bronze blades with a large copper ball set in the center. They are very heavy, and suffer a -2 to attack rolls. However, once they are thrown, they explode upon touching anything substantial and deal 4d6 Fire damage within 10 feet. Survivors within 20 feet are deafened for 1d6 rounds. 0.5lb each (Source: /u/LittleDizzleDaGusha)

items.name: The Blaze of Glory
items.note: A heavy, simple platinum torch that will not ignite under normal circumstances. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: The Olm Shield
items.note: A circular shield with an amber edge, and covered in a lustrous, gold-colored metal. On the face of the shield is a depiction of a man destroying a dragon while the dragon destroys the man, as in an ouroboros. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Thunderhead Bolts
items.note: Obviously mystical arrows of superior make. They have a metallic shaft etched with elegant marks of clouds and storms, and always glow with a pale shimmer. The markings occasionally gleam with bright light. A full set is 13 arrows or bolts. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Winged Eye
items.note: This extremely tiny Beholder creature has wings in addition to its ten eyed tentacles. It is made of delicate glass that is finely stained. Any arcane spellcaster can activate it, as can any creature that succeeds on a DC 20 Use Magic Device skill check. When activated, it will come to life in all respects—except that it is tethered to the mind of the activator. It cannot attack, move objects, or affect the physical setting in any way. However, it can fly up to 100 ft away at a speed of 10 ft per round. The activator can now see as though their head were the eye, and can ONLY see what the eye would see. This means the original PC is temporarily “blinded.” The eye can be deactived at any time from any distance within 100 ft. However, as soon as it is deactived, it turns back to delicate glass—wherever it was. Thus deactivating it before flying it back is not recommended. (Source: /u/LittleDizzleDaGusha)

items.name: Zoltar's Pearlescent Ring
items.note: A simple white pearlescent ring. Curse: Lose 1d4 max health once per day. The effect stacks each day. Once worn, this ring cannot be removed. Any attempt to remove the ring sees it reappear after a few seconds. Any attempt to circumvent the curse by removing the ring finger sees it appear on a different finger. If the wearer removes every finger, the curse ends. Should they fail to get the curse removed before their health falls below 0 they become an undead thrall. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: 48-sided Garnet Gemstone
items.note: Garnet is a general class of crystals ranging from deep red to violet in colour. a typical cut for this rare isometric gem is 12 to 24 sides, with the very rare 36 sides being known from time to time. This may be the only 48 sided garnet in existance. and as such it is highly prized. (Source: BGII)

items.name: Apparatus of Kwalish
items.note: An apparatus of Kwalish appears to be a large, sealed iron barrel, but it has a secret catch that opens a hatch in one end. Inside, a character finds ten levers. The levers perform various functions, including extending or retracting the legs, tail, pincers and feelers, covering and uncovering the portholes, moving the apparatus, snapping its pincers, opening and closing its magically-lit "eyes", rising and sinking in water, and opening or closing the hatch. When activated the apparatus looks like a giant lobster. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Apple of the Gods
items.note: Apple of the God’s – gain random ability of 1 god aspect at risk of save vs death, then random forbidden fruit side effect; ability is 1 per day/50% chance success (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Armor +3
items.note: You have a +3 bonus to AC while wearing this armor. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Armor of Invulnerability
items.note: You have resistance to nonmagical damage while you wear this armor. Additionally, you can use an action to make yourself immune to nonmagical damage for 10 minutes or until you are no longer wearing the armor. Once this special action is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Balering, the Sword of Balance
items.note: A long steel blade with an amber edge and lustrous golden hilt. When first discovered, the blade is without detail, but is filled with runes as members of other races volunteer their blood as tribute to the blade's cause. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Belt of Cloud Giant Strength
items.note:
While wearing this belt, your Strength score changes to 27. If your Strength is already
equal to or greater than 27, the item has no effect on you. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Belt of Storm Giant Strength
items.note:
While wearing this belt, your Strength score changes to 29. If your Strength is already
equal to or greater than 29, the item has no effect on you. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Bloodgaze, Champion of Vingaret
items.note: This greatsword belongs to the current mortal champion of Vingaret, the goddess of vengeance. (Source: Scribnibbler21)

items.name: Bound Armor of the Demon
items.note: Gives insane buffs, will eventually turn you into one of whatever it's namesake is. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Bound Armor of the Fey
items.note: Gives insane buffs, will eventually turn you into one of whatever it's namesake is. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Bound Armor of the Lich
items.note: Gives insane buffs, will eventually turn you into one of whatever it's namesake is. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Brass Halo
items.note: A small brass flame-shaped charm on a black cord. The center of the flame is overlaid with platinum and is visible in darkness. Holding it in your hands fills you with a tingling sense of ambition and an urge to act. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Bronze Halo
items.note: A flattened bronze halo that hovers behind the bearer's head. It spins rapidly when the bearer exhibits great presence of mind. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Cloak of the Shadow General
items.note: A commanding cloak of authority, used to hide whole legions of armies against the Starwizards of the astral planes. (Source: u/giratina2648)

items.name: Copper Band of the Deceiver
items.note: A copper band with a scale-pattern etched into it. At the top of the bracelet is a broken inset where a platinum decoration used to be. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Copper Halo
items.note: A copper gauntlet that never greens. The bearer feels a sense of influence and importance. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Eternity Armor
items.note:
Nigh-unbreakable golemsteel armor enchanted to withstand nearly any strike.
If a ranged attack targets a friendly creature besides yourself within five feet radius, you can move them and take the shot to negate the damage and effects instead; if they are within a 10 feet radius, use your reaction. (Source: u/giratina2648)

items.name: Golden Halo
items.note: A necklace of large gold-and-platinum prayer beads, 12 beads in all. They project a feeling of safety and austere silence. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Hallowed Skin
items.note: A thick, flesh-like leather vest adorned with occult symbols and human teeth. Parts of it are ragged with wear, and it feels clammy to the touch. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Observer's Ring
items.note: This ring is set with a hemispherical glass eyeball that looks around when it is worn. It functions as an alternative eye for the user and is not affected by magic.

items.name: Perpetual Tree
items.note: The sapling of a young tree. Within a few minutes it will have grown into a wizened oak. A few moments more and the tree slowly fades from existence as a young seedling pushes up from the ground and begins the cycle anew.

items.name: Pocket Tornado
items.note: Two disks of hardened quicksilver laying atop one another. If they are separated, a tornado springs up between them – anchored on each end to a disk.

items.name: Potion of Temporary Immortality
items.note: Bottle with a liquid with rainbow colors. When you drink it you automatically succeed on death saving throws for one hour. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Seaglass Earring
items.note: A single, elegant seaglass colored earring made of clear glasslike material. Its exact shape changes slowly over time, shifting from one watery artful shape to another. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Wolfpack
items.note: This backpack is an object of primal craftsmanship, fashioned entirely from the cured pelt of an old alpha wolf. The functional opening of the backpack is the wolf's actual head. To access the contents, the wearer must unfasten an invisible latch beneath the lower jaw. When opened, the powerful jaws part, revealing a surprisingly spacious, leather-lined compartment within the throat and chest cavity of the pelt. Securing the pack involves a satisfying, sharp snap as the upper and lower jaws meet and lock, looking from a distance as if the beast is simply resting its maw shut, perhaps in a silent snarl that wards off thieves. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Handy Haversack
items.note: Source: PHB

items.name: Silver Halo
items.note: A small silver ring shaped from the circle of Captain Wellby's hat. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Smoldering Cloak
items.note: A black and crimson cloak with tattered edges that are ever-glowing as it were on fire on moments ago. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Solarion
items.note: A simple, sturdy longsword with a broad blade. There is a shining sun sigil at the crossguard. The blade shines as if reflecting sunlight, even in the dimmest rooms. (Source: /u/gimme_some_sunshine)

items.name: Soul Stealer
items.note: A legendary longsword that glows a dark red when a creature dies within 10 feet of the blade. (Source: William Orfanakos)

items.name: Thaxton's Hammer
items.note: This warhammer belonged to a famous dwarven general known as Thaxton the Fierce. It grants a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls, as well as Intimidation checks, to the user. If the user is a dwarf, this bonus is increased to +2. (Source: Scribnibbler21)

items.name: The Book of Kaza
items.note: The Book of Kaza is a famed tome written by the lich, Kaza. It is said to deal with the darkest secrets of necromancy, though the pages appear to be blank when you look at it. Kaza was destroyed during the upheaval of Nethril's final days. How it has come to its present location after so many years is anyone's guess. (Source: BGII)

items.name: The Box of 'Do Want'
items.note: "The Box of Do Want" which made anyone looking into the box desire whatever it was that I put inside of it. A rock? The shopkeeper wants it so badly they're willing to pay their entire fortune. A spoon? The tavern keeper is willing to give you his daughter. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: The Eye of Far Seeing
items.note: This legendary eyeball, said to have once been that of a gynosphinx, has the capability to scry at a radius of around 1000 miles. To use it, one must attune to it and to do so, one must gouge out one of their own eyes and replace it with the Eye of Far Seeing (similar to the eye of vecna). (Source: J)

items.name: Vial #2
items.note: Vial #2 contains speculumia, a slightly greenish opaque liquid that when drunk suddenly causes a cloud of smoke and summons an exact copy of whoever drank it. The new person is completely the same as the drinker and will actually think that he is the one who drank the drink, the sudden cloud of smoke will prevent anyone witnessing the situation from being able to tell which one is the original person.

items.name: Dwarven Urgrosh
items.note: A versatile polearm that combines a long-reaching spear tip for thrusting with a heavy axe blade for chopping. Originally used exclusively for mining, these weapons were adapted to combat creatures in the Underdark. (Source: Uberbeard)
